functions.comment: Active-player counter identified by the lazy `sv_maxclients` cache, the CS_PLAYERS configstring scan, the non-empty name gate, and the spectator exclusion from ai_chat.c::BotNumActivePlayers.

Ranking predicate identified by the mirrored active-player sweep from `BotIsFirstInRankings`, but with the inverse score comparison that returns false when another live player scores lower, matching ai_chat.c::BotIsLastInRankings.

Top-ranked client-name helper identified by the cached `sv_maxclients` scan, the best-score accumulator seeded to a large negative value, and the final `EasyClientName` fill of the static name buffer from ai_chat.c::BotFirstClientInRankings.

Bottom-ranked client-name helper identified by the mirrored score scan from `BotFirstClientInRankings`, the large positive worst-score seed, and the final `EasyClientName` return buffer from ai_chat.c::BotLastClientInRankings.

Random-opponent helper identified by the candidate array of non-spectator non-teammate clients, the `BotSameTeam` exclusion, the random index selection, and the final `EasyClientName` return from ai_chat.c::BotRandomOpponentName.
Map-title helper identified by the `trap_GetServerinfo` call, the `mapname` info-key lookup, and the copy into a static 128-byte buffer from ai_chat.c::BotMapTitle.

Means-of-death string helper identified by the switch returning `Shotgun`, `Gauntlet`, `Rocket Launcher`, `Plasmagun`, `Railgun`, `Lightning Gun`, `BFG10K`, `Nailgun`, `Chaingun`, `Proximity Launcher`, `Kamikaze`, `Prox mine`, `Grapple`, and `[unknown weapon]`, matching ai_chat.c::BotWeaponNameForMeansOfDeath.

Random-weapon helper identified by the `random() * 11.9` style bucket selection and the return set of Gauntlet through Proximity Launcher with `BFG10K` as the default, matching ai_chat.c::BotRandomWeaponName.

Enemy-visibility scan identified by the loop over client slots, the `BotEntityInfo` validity gate, dead or invisible-or-not-shooting rejection, `BotSameTeam` exclusion, and the final `BotEntityVisible` test from ai_chat.c::BotVisibleEnemies.

Chat-position gate identified by the dead-player early return, the powerup exclusions, the lava or slime and water point-content probes, and the ground trace that requires standing on `ENTITYNUM_WORLD`, matching ai_chat.c::BotValidChatPosition.

Enter-game chat handler identified by the `game_enter` initial-chat key, the non-teamplay and non-tournament gating, and the payload built from `EasyClientName`, `BotRandomOpponentName`, and `BotMapTitle`, matching ai_chat.c::BotChat_EnterGame.

Exit-game chat handler identified by the `game_exit` initial-chat key, the shared enter-or-exit chat characteristic gate, and the matching `EasyClientName`/`BotRandomOpponentName`/`BotMapTitle` payload setup from ai_chat.c::BotChat_ExitGame.

Start-level chat handler identified by the observer and teamplay gates, the `vtaunt` fallback in team modes, and the `level_start` initial-chat key with `EasyClientName(bs->client, ...)`, matching ai_chat.c::BotChat_StartLevel.

End-level chat handler identified by the teamplay `vtaunt` branch when the bot is first in rankings, the `level_end_victory` / `level_end_lose` / `level_end` chat-key split, and the matching `BotIsFirstInRankings`, `BotIsLastInRankings`, `BotFirstClientInRankings`, `BotLastClientInRankings`, and `BotMapTitle` payload flow from ai_chat.c::BotChat_EndLevel.

Enemy-suicide chat handler identified by the `enemy_suicide` initial-chat key, the non-teamplay and non-tournament gating, and the surrounding `BotValidChatPosition` plus `BotVisibleEnemies` checks from ai_chat.c::BotChat_EnemySuicide.

Hit-without-dying chat handler identified by the `lasthurt_client` validation, the `hit_nodeath` initial-chat key, the `EntityIsShooting` rejection on the current enemy, and the paired `ClientName` plus `BotWeaponNameForMeansOfDeath(lasthurt_mod)` payload from ai_chat.c::BotChat_HitNoDeath.

Hit-without-killing chat handler identified by the `hit_nokill` initial-chat key, the `BotValidChatPosition` and `BotVisibleEnemies` gates, the `EntityIsShooting` rejection on the current enemy, and the final `ClientName(bs->enemy, ...)` plus means-of-death payload from ai_chat.c::BotChat_HitNoKill.
Chat-duration helper identified by the sole `CHARACTERISTIC_CHAT_CPM` query and the retail constant `2.0` return path from ai_chat.c::BotChatTime.

Death-chat handler identified by the death_drown/death_slime/death_lava/death_cratered/death_suicide/death_telefrag/death_kamikaze/death_gauntlet/death_rail/death_bfg/death_insult/death_praise chat-key fan-out and the matching teamplay/lastkilledby control flow from ai_chat.c::BotChat_Death.

Kill-chat handler identified by the kill_teammate/kill_gauntlet/kill_rail/kill_telefrag/kill_kamikaze/kill_insult/kill_praise branches and the surrounding BotValidChatPosition/BotVisibleEnemies gates from ai_chat.c::BotChat_Kill.

Random chat selector identified by the random_misc/random_insult split, the LTG_TEAMHELP/LTG_TEAMACCOMPANY/LTG_RUSHBASE exclusions, and the matching BotRandomWeaponName/BotMapTitle payload setup from ai_chat.c::BotChat_Random.
Bot chat exerciser anchored by the preserved game_enter/game_exit/level_start and death_* initial-chat keys; the retail body iterates those scripted chats exactly like ai_chat.c::BotChatTest.

Exact team-goal item resolver from ai_cmd.c::BotGetItemTeamGoal. It rejects empty goal names, walks `trap_BotGetLevelItemGoal`, skips dropped items via `GFL_DROPPED`, and returns the first persistent level-item goal that matches the requested name.

Exact message-goal resolver from ai_cmd.c::BotGetMessageTeamGoal. It first reuses `BotGetItemTeamGoal`, then falls back to the waypoint list under `bs->checkpoints`, copying the matched waypoint goal into the caller's output buffer.

Exact time-parser from ai_cmd.c::BotGetTime. The retail body gates on `ST_TIME`, extracts the TIME token, resolves `MTCONTEXT_TIME`, handles the `forever` / `for a while` / `for a long time` aliases, and otherwise converts minute or second payloads into an absolute `FloatTime()` deadline.

Client-name lookup helper identified by the lazy `sv_maxclients` cache, the exact ClientName/Q_stricmp pass over live client slots, and the fallback substring pass via stristr from ai_cmd.c::FindClientByName.

Enemy-name lookup helper identified by the lazy `sv_maxclients` cache, the BotSameTeam exclusion on both exact and substring scans, and the matching ClientName-based return of the first enemy client from ai_cmd.c::FindEnemyByName.

Same-team roster counter identified by the lazy `sv_maxclients` cache, the CS_PLAYERS configstring non-empty gate, and the repeated same-team compare between the bot client and each candidate slot from ai_cmd.c::NumPlayersOnSameTeam.
Patrol waypoint parser identified by the literal "what do you say?" fallback, repeated KEYAREA/MORE matching, and the new-waypoint chain construction that mirrors ai_cmd.c::BotGetPatrolWaypoints.

Address-resolution helper identified by the NETNAME/ADDRESSEE parsing, the multiple-name/everyone handling, the subteam/team-name checks, and the random non-tell throttling logic from ai_cmd.c::BotAddressedToBot.

Team order handler identified by the whois/whereareyou fallbacks, teammate goal acquisition, and the LTG_TEAMHELP versus LTG_TEAMACCOMPANY split with formation setup from ai_cmd.c::BotMatch_HelpAccompany.
Defend-order handler identified by KEYAREA team-goal lookup, TEAM_DEFENDKEYAREA timing, and the LTG_DEFENDKEYAREA setup path from ai_cmd.c::BotMatch_DefendKeyArea.
Get-item order handler identified by ITEM goal parsing, TEAM_GETITEM_TIME scheduling, and the LTG_GETITEM state transition from ai_cmd.c::BotMatch_GetItem.
Camp-order handler identified by the whois and whereareyou fallbacks, the ST_THERE/ST_HERE camp-goal branches, and the LTG_CAMPORDER timing path from ai_cmd.c::BotMatch_Camp.
Patrol-order handler identified by the BotGetPatrolWaypoints call, TEAM_PATROL_TIME scheduling, and the LTG_PATROL setup from ai_cmd.c::BotMatch_Patrol.
CTF get-flag order handler identified by the ctf_redflag/ctf_blueflag/ctf_neutralflag availability checks, alternate-route setup, and the LTG_GETFLAG timing path from ai_cmd.c::BotMatch_GetFlag.
Attack-enemy-base order handler identified by the CTF handoff into BotMatch_GetFlag, the obelisk-area gating, and the LTG_ATTACKENEMYBASE setup from ai_cmd.c::BotMatch_AttackEnemyBase.
Harvester order handler identified by the neutral/red/blue obelisk availability checks and the LTG_HARVEST timing path from ai_cmd.c::BotMatch_Harvest.
Rush-base order handler identified by the CTF/1FCTF/Harvester gametype gating, CTF_RUSHBASE_TIME scheduling, and the LTG_RUSHBASE setup from ai_cmd.c::BotMatch_RushBase.
Task-preference handler identified by the defender/attacker/roamer subtype mask updates and the trailing keepinmind tell from ai_cmd.c::BotMatch_TaskPreference.
Return-flag order handler identified by the CTF/1FCTF gating, CTF_RETURNFLAG_TIME scheduling, and the LTG_RETURNFLAG state setup from ai_cmd.c::BotMatch_ReturnFlag.
Join-subteam handler identified by the joinedteam chat key, TEAMNAME copy into bs->subteam, and the matching tell reply from ai_cmd.c::BotMatch_JoinSubteam.
Leave-subteam handler identified by the leftteam chat key, the bs->subteam clear, and the tell reply back to the caller from ai_cmd.c::BotMatch_LeaveSubteam.

Dismiss handler identified by the dismissed chat key, ltg/lead reset, and decisionmaker update path from ai_cmd.c::BotMatch_Dismiss.

Team-leader start handler identified by the ST_I versus TEAMMATE parsing split and the copy into the bs->teamleader string from ai_cmd.c::BotMatch_StartTeamLeaderShip.

Who-is-team-leader responder identified by the literal "I'm the team leader\n" team say and the bs->teamleader comparison from ai_cmd.c::BotMatch_WhoIsTeamLeader.

Central team-chat dispatcher identified by the unknown match type fallback, the formation-damage placeholder text, and the exact switch fan-out over the ai_cmd.c BotMatch_* handlers.

Primary combat node recovered from the exact battle fight debug strings for enemy-dead, enemy-out-of-sight, invisible, and chat side paths; the retail flow matches ai_dmnet.c::AINode_Battle_Fight.

Retail-only team tutorial enter helper that points the bot at the adjacent lead teammate node and preserves the lead teammate state label from the retail bot tutorial flow.

Retail-only taunt-heavy tutorial node anchored by the exact torment human observer/no ai node strings and the preserved say Kill me if you can - fragbait! chat branch in the human-harassment state.

Descriptive retail-only late tutorial enter helper. `BotDeathmatchAI` enters this state when the recovered training phase selector reaches `3`, and the body resets the late-training timers and flags, reapplies the tutorial bot-state bits, and records the node switch under the retail `lead teammate` label before handing control to the adjacent fragbait / return node.

Descriptive retail-only late tutorial lead node. It reuses the `lead teammate: observer` / `intermission` / `bot dead` / `no ai node` / `no client` exits, drives the preserved `say Kill me if you can - fragbait!` and `say Well done! Let's get back to the tutorial. Follow me.` chat branches, and routes through `BotGetLocalClient`, `BotSetTrainingBotState`, and the adjacent point-facing helper while the tutorial bot alternates between fragbaiting and escorting the local player back into the script.
Retail-only enter helper for the instant-rail tutorial mode. HLIL shows it assigning the adjacent insta-gib node and preserving the insta gib! label from the bot state machine.
Retail-only instagib tutorial node identified by the exact insta gib! observer/no ai node/found enemy strings and the surrounding rail-only combat state transitions.

Descriptive retail-only instagib goal getter. It tries `BotRefreshInstaGibTargetGoal`, falls back to a fresh nearest-target scan when needed, seeds the instagib LTG timers and target entity number, and finally copies the cached goal slab out to the caller.

Exact match for ai_team.c::BotCTFOrders_BothFlagsAtBase. The passive and aggressive branches preserve the exact defend/getflag team-size splits used when both flags are still on their stands.
Exact match for ai_team.c::BotCTFOrders. The dispatcher computes the same combined flagstatus from team-relative red/blue flag states and fans out into the four CTF order helpers.

Exact match for ai_team.c::BotCreateGroup. It treats teammates[0] as the leader, emits cmd_accompany or cmd_accompanyme for the rest of the group, and routes those orders through BotSayTeamOrderAlways.

Exact match for ai_team.c::BotTeamOrders. The retail helper enumerates same-team non-spectators and builds the same 2-player and 3-player follow groups that the GPL version creates via BotCreateGroup.
Exact match for ai_team.c::Bot1FCTFOrders_FlagAtCenter. The retail flow preserves the passive 50/40 and aggressive 30/60 defend/getflag splits for the center-flag state.
Exact match for ai_team.c::Bot1FCTFOrders_TeamHasFlag. The retail branches preserve cmd_attackenemybase, defendbase, and accompany/me follow orders as the team escorts the neutral-flag carrier home.

Exact match for ai_team.c::Bot1FCTFOrders_EnemyHasFlag. The passive and aggressive branches preserve the heavy defendbase emphasis and the cmd_returnflag assignments used when the enemy controls the neutral flag.

Exact match for ai_team.c::Bot1FCTFOrders_EnemyDroppedFlag. The retail body reuses the expected defendbase/getflag split logic for the dropped neutral-flag state and matches the source percentages in both strategy branches.
Exact match for ai_team.c::Bot1FCTFOrders. The dispatcher switches on neutralflagstatus and hands control to the four 1FCTF order helpers in the same order as the source.
Exact match for ai_team.c::BotObeliskOrders. The retail body preserves the defendbase versus attackenemybase split ratios and voice orders used in Overload-style obelisk play.
Exact match for ai_team.c::BotHarvesterOrders, anchored by the repeated cmd_harvest orders and the passive/aggressive defend-versus-harvest percentage splits from the GPL source.

Exact match for ai_team.c::FindHumanTeamLeader. The retail scan walks live non-bot teammates that have not opted out via notleader, copies the selected leader name, and defaults that player to a defend voice order when no prior task exists.
Teamplay coordinator anchored by the iamteamleader/whoisteamleader/startleader strings and the matching ai_team.c logic that elects a leader and emits team orders through vsay_team.

functions.signature: void BotVoiceChat_GetFlag(bot_state_t *bs, int client, int mode)
functions.comment: Exact voice-command handler from ai_vcmd.c. The retail `getflag` table slot validates the active CTF-style flag goals, marks the bot ordered by `client`, sets `LTG_GETFLAG`, and seeds the alternate-route request toward the enemy base.

functions.signature: void BotVoiceChat_Offense(bot_state_t *bs, int client, int mode)
functions.comment: Exact voice-command offense handler from ai_vcmd.c. Retail forwards CTF and 1FCTF straight into `BotVoiceChat_GetFlag`, uses `LTG_HARVEST` for Harvester, and otherwise assigns the attack-enemy-base long-term goal.

functions.signature: void BotVoiceChat_Defend(bot_state_t *bs, int client, int mode)
functions.comment: Exact voice-command defend handler from ai_vcmd.c. The retail body selects the home obelisk or flag goal for the bot team, marks the bot ordered by `client`, and assigns the `LTG_DEFENDKEYAREA` task.

functions.signature: void BotVoiceChat_DefendFlag(bot_state_t *bs, int client, int mode)
functions.comment: Exact trivial `defendflag` wrapper from ai_vcmd.c. The retail voice-command table points that token at a tailcall thunk into `BotVoiceChat_Defend`, matching the GPL wrapper.

functions.signature: void BotVoiceChat_Patrol(bot_state_t *bs, int client, int mode)
functions.comment: Exact patrol-order handler from ai_vcmd.c. Retail clears the current long-term team task, emits the `dismissed` chat plus `onpatrol` voice response, and refreshes the cached team-status slab.

functions.signature: void BotVoiceChat_Camp(bot_state_t *bs, int client, int mode)
functions.comment: Exact camp-order handler from ai_vcmd.c. It snapshots the requesting teammate into `bs->teamgoal` when a reachable PVS position is available, otherwise asks `whereareyou`, and on success assigns the `LTG_CAMPORDER` task.

functions.signature: void BotVoiceChat_FollowMe(bot_state_t *bs, int client, int mode)
functions.comment: Exact accompany-order handler from ai_vcmd.c. Retail snapshots the teammate goal when visible, otherwise asks `whereareyou`, then assigns `LTG_TEAMACCOMPANY`, resets arrival state, and seeds the 3.5-meter formation distance.
Exact flag-carrier escort handler from ai_vcmd.c. It scans live teammates for the active flag carrier and forwards the order into `BotVoiceChat_FollowMe` once one is found.

functions.signature: void BotVoiceChat_ReturnFlag(bot_state_t *bs, int client, int mode)
functions.comment: Exact return-flag order handler from ai_vcmd.c. The retail body is gated to CTF and 1FCTF, marks the bot ordered by `client`, sets `LTG_RETURNFLAG`, and clears the rush-base timeout.

functions.signature: void BotVoiceChat_StartLeader(bot_state_t *bs, int client, int mode)
functions.comment: Exact tiny leader-start wrapper from ai_vcmd.c. The retail `startleader` table slot is just the bounded client-name copy into `bs->teamleader`.

functions.signature: void BotVoiceChat_StopLeader(bot_state_t *bs, int client, int mode)
functions.comment: Exact leader-stop handler from ai_vcmd.c. Retail resolves the named client, compares that name against `bs->teamleader`, and on match clears the leader string and marks the client as unwilling to lead.

functions.signature: void BotVoiceChat_WhoIsLeader(bot_state_t *bs, int client, int mode)
functions.comment: Exact leader-query response handler from ai_vcmd.c. When team play is active and the bot's own name matches `bs->teamleader`, retail emits the `iamteamleader` chat and the `startleader` voice cue to the team.

functions.signature: void BotVoiceChat_WantOnDefense(bot_state_t *bs, int client, int mode)
functions.comment: Exact defense-preference handler from ai_vcmd.c. It flips the teammate preference bits toward `TEAMTP_DEFENDER`, replies with the `keepinmind` chat, sends the `yes` voice response, and triggers the affirmative action gesture.

functions.signature: void BotVoiceChat_WantOnOffense(bot_state_t *bs, int client, int mode)
functions.comment: Exact offense-preference handler from ai_vcmd.c. Retail mirrors `BotVoiceChat_WantOnDefense`, but flips the teammate preference bits toward `TEAMTP_ATTACKER` before sending the same `keepinmind` acknowledgment and affirmative response.

Exact voice-chat parser and dispatcher from ai_vcmd.c. The retail body rejects SAY_ALL and cross-team senders, parses the `voiceOnly clientNum color command` preamble, and dispatches the command token through the interleaved `voiceCommands` table.

Descriptive retail-only warmup/readyup predicate. HLIL shows this pure helper returning false for FFA, Duel, Race, and Red Rover, and true for the remaining team-side readyup modes that require both teams to exist before the stricter denial path is used.

Descriptive retail-only gametype label helper. The switch returns the compact HUD/report tags `FFA`, `DUEL`, `RACE`, `TDM`, `CA`, `CTF`, `1F`, `OB`, `HAR`, `FT`, `DOM`, `AD`, and `RR`, with `ERR` as the fallback.
Exact pure team-name helper from g_team.c::TeamName. It returns RED, BLUE, SPECTATOR, or FREE for TEAM_RED, TEAM_BLUE, TEAM_SPECTATOR, and all other team values.
Exact pure team-color helper from g_team.c::TeamColorString. Retail returns `^1` for red, `^4` for blue, and `^7` for every other team value, including spectators.

Descriptive retail-only split predicate beneath `G_PowerupCarrierKillColorPrefix`. HLIL reduces it to the tiny objective-gametype classifier the adjacent carrier-kill announcer uses to decide whether the printed powerup label should keep the white prefix instead of the yellow fallback.

Exact round-controller gametype predicate from the retail CA/A&D/Freeze/Red Rover seam. The binary keeps it as a tiny pure classifier over the current `g_gametype` value, and multiple callers reuse it to gate round-based spawn, AFK, and rankings behavior.

Descriptive retail-only shared flag-item predicate. HLIL reduces the body to a null/type guard on `IT_TEAM` plus a three-way `PW_REDFLAG` / `PW_BLUEFLAG` / `PW_NEUTRALFLAG` tag check, and retail reuses it in both the dropped-flag pickup reach logic and the team-item spawn/configstring path.
Exact team-swap helper from g_team.c::OtherTeam. It flips red to blue, blue to red, and leaves all other team values unchanged.
Retail-only generic item-table lookup helper. HLIL shows it scanning `bg_itemlist` for a matching `giType` plus `giTag` pair and raising `Couldn't find item for type %i tag %i` on failure.
Exact powerup lookup helper from bg_misc.c::BG_FindItemForPowerup. The retail body preserves the invulnerability holdable remap, the TA-rune branch, and the final shared powerup or team-item scan.

Descriptive retail-only race time formatter. It converts signed milliseconds into an absolute minute-plus-seconds value, keeps the sign prefix when needed, zero-pads sub-10 second remainders, and returns the formatted `%s%d:%s%.03f` string through the shared varargs buffer.

Exact pickup-name lookup helper from bg_misc.c::BG_FindItem. Retail walks `bg_itemlist`, compares the requested pickup name against each item's display name with `Q_stricmp`, and returns the matching `gitem_t *` or NULL.

Descriptive retail-only weapon-name string helper. HLIL reduces the body to a bounds check over the shared retail weapon-name table with a `None` fallback, and the adjacent `G_ExpandChatTokens` path uses it to expand the `#w` token into the current weapon label plus ammo count; source reconstruction maps the local weapon enum through the retail item-tag order so Heavy Machinegun still resolves through item tag 14.

Exact touch-bounds helper from bg_misc.c::BG_PlayerTouchesItem. Binary Ninja HLIL evaluates the item trajectory, keeps the retail +/-36 horizontal bounds and -50 lower vertical tolerance, uses a 29-unit upward window for normal items, and expands the upward window to 64 units for red, blue, and neutral team-flag items.

functions.signature: static qboolean BG_CanGrabArmorItem(int gametype, int currentTime, const entityState_t *ent, const playerState_t *ps, const gitem_t *item, qboolean dropped)
functions.comment: Standalone retail tiered armor-pickup helper used from `BG_CanItemBeGrabbed`. Binary Ninja HLIL shows the 100/50/25 item-quantity branches: red armor allows current armor below 200, yellow armor allows below 150 through tier 1 or `armor * 0.75 <= 99.0` at tier 2, and green armor allows below 100 at tier 0, `armor * 0.66 <= 50.0` at tier 1, or `armor * 0.75 <= 50.0` at tier 2.

functions.signature: static qboolean BG_CanGrabWeaponItem(int gametype, int currentTime, const entityState_t *ent, const playerState_t *ps, const gitem_t *item, qboolean dropped)
functions.comment: Exact weapon-pickup helper from bg_misc.c::BG_CanGrabWeaponItem. HLIL shows the early `ps->pm_flags & PMF_IRONSIGHTS` rejection followed by the dropped-item, missing-weapon, zero-ammo, and duplicate-weapon checks.

functions.signature: void BG_EvaluateTrajectory(const trajectory_t *tr, int atTime, vec3_t result)
functions.comment: Exact trajectory evaluator from bg_misc.c::BG_EvaluateTrajectory. The retail switch covers stationary, interpolate, linear, linear-stop, sine, gravity, and the Quake Live TR_QL_ACCEL type-6 path, where the dword after trDelta supplies the extra acceleration scalar before unknown trajectory types fault.

functions.signature: void BG_EvaluateTrajectoryDelta(const trajectory_t *tr, int atTime, vec3_t result)
functions.comment: Exact trajectory-velocity evaluator from bg_misc.c::BG_EvaluateTrajectoryDelta. The retail body preserves the sine derivative path, the linear-stop zeroing after expiry, the gravity Z subtraction, and the TR_QL_ACCEL type-6 delta path that subtracts the extra acceleration scalar times elapsed seconds.

Exact playerstate-to-entitystate bridge from bg_misc.c::BG_PlayerStateToEntityState. The body mirrors the pm_type and health driven eType selection, the origin and velocity copies, and the full event, animation, powerup, and client-state replication.

Descriptive tiny gametype predicate used by the ClientSpawn-side bootstrap before the late `G_UseTargets` spawn-point dispatch. HLIL preserves the full truth table: it returns false only for `GT_RACE`, `GT_CLAN_ARENA`, `GT_DOMINATION`, and `GT_RED_ROVER`, and true for every other retail gametype value.

Exact jump-pad touch helper from bg_misc.c::BG_TouchJumpPad. Retail keeps the spectator and flight early-outs, suppresses duplicate triggers for the same pad, emits the shared jump-pad predictable event with parm 0, and then copies the pad velocity into the playerstate.

Exact vector-printer helper from g_utils.c::vtos. Retail rotates through the same eight-slot static buffer ring and formats the incoming vector as `(%.2f %.2f %.2f)`, which is slightly more precise than the current GPL integer formatter.

Descriptive retail-only spawn-filter parser. HLIL shows it tokenizing the incoming `gametype` / `not_gametype` string, matching each token against the Quake Live gametype name table from `FFA` through `RR`, and accumulating the per-gametype bitmask later tested by the worldspawn entity gate.

Exact disable-loadout parser from g_spawn.c::G_ParseDisableLoadoutString. Retail tokenizes the map or cvar string, walks the shared loadout-token table, and ORs the corresponding weapon/item bits into the final mask before publishing configstrings.

Deliberate synthetic retail helper at the top of `PmoveSingle`. The body selects one of two small constant bundles based on the current movement flag profile and seeds the shared pmove globals consumed by jump, air, and water movement later in the frame.
Exact predictable-event wrapper from bg_pmove.c::PM_AddEvent. It appends the supplied event to `pm->ps` with an event parm of 0.

Exact touch-entity accumulator from bg_pmove.c::PM_AddTouchEnt. Retail skips `ENTITYNUM_WORLD`, caps the list at `MAXTOUCH`, and preserves the duplicate-suppression scan before appending a new touched entity.
Exact torso-animation starter from bg_pmove.c::PM_StartTorsoAnim. The retail helper only checks `pm_type < PM_DEAD`, then flips the toggle bit and writes the requested torso animation.

Exact ready-weapon torso idler from bg_pmove.c::PM_TorsoAnimation. HLIL shows the ready-state gate, the gauntlet-specific `TORSO_STAND2` selection, and the default `TORSO_STAND` continuation for the remaining weapons.

Exact low-priority legs-animation helper from bg_pmove.c::PM_ContinueLegsAnim. Retail returns early when the current legs anim already matches or a legs timer is active, otherwise it toggles into the requested animation.
Exact forced legs-animation helper from bg_pmove.c::PM_ForceLegsAnim. It clears the legs timer and then toggles in the requested legs animation if the player is still below `PM_DEAD`.

functions.signature: void PM_ClipVelocity(vec3_t in, vec3_t normal, vec3_t out, float overbounce)
functions.comment: Exact collision-response helper from bg_pmove.c::PM_ClipVelocity. It computes the overbounce-adjusted backoff and subtracts the normal-projected change from each axis of the input velocity.

Exact friction helper from bg_pmove.c::PM_Friction. The retail body zeroes tiny speeds, applies grounded friction with crouch-slide and diagonal circle-strafe overrides, adds water/flight/spectator drops, applies the PM_FREEZE or PMF_SCOREBOARD 0.25 drop multiplier, then rescales all three velocity components by the resulting newspeed fraction.

functions.signature: static void PM_Accelerate(vec3_t wishdir, float wishspeed, float accel)
functions.comment: Exact acceleration helper from bg_pmove.c::PM_Accelerate. Retail computes current speed along the wish direction, clamps the added speed by the accel and frametime budget, and pushes velocity along the normalized wish direction.

Exact command-scaling helper from bg_pmove.c::PM_CmdScale. It finds the largest axial input component, computes the total movement vector length, and returns the speed-preserving scale factor for the `-127..127` command range.

Deliberate synthetic retail helper shared by the fly, water, ladder, and noclip paths. HLIL applies the active command scale to the current forward/right/up basis, adds the vertical input component, normalizes the resulting 3D wish vector in place, and returns the derived wishspeed.

Exact legs-facing helper from bg_pmove.c::PM_SetMovementDir. The retail ladder maps forward and strafe combinations to the eight movement directions and snaps idle sideways cases back to the diagonal poses.

Descriptive retail-only jump takeoff body shared by PM_CheckJump and the step-jump seam. HLIL shows it latching the jump flags, deriving the final vertical takeoff velocity from the active jump-mode globals, emitting `EV_JUMP`, selecting the forward or backward legs animation, and refreshing the jump timestamp used by the surrounding gates.

Descriptive retail-only crouch step-jump gate checked from the PM_StepSlideMove seam. The leaf requires the ducked movement flag, rejects the pending step-jump suppression latch, keeps only non-descending vertical velocity, and reuses the same jump-cooldown timing threshold as the adjacent jump helpers before reporting success.

Descriptive retail-only step-jump precheck beneath PM_StepSlideMove. It enforces the retail upmove, jump-delay, and jump-held restrictions around the step-jump modes, clearing the queued jump state on rejected attempts before the surrounding movement code decides whether to run the takeoff body.

Exact jump gate from bg_pmove.c::PM_CheckJump. The retail helper enforces the jump-held and timing checks, handles the air double-jump allowance, and then dispatches into the adjacent takeoff body when a jump is accepted.

functions.signature: qboolean BG_CanItemBeGrabbed(int gametype, int currentTime, const entityState_t *ent, const playerState_t *ps)
functions.comment: Exact match for bg_misc.c::BG_CanItemBeGrabbed, anchored by the index out of range and invalid item type diagnostics plus the shared item-type switch that gates armor, health, ammo, team items, and dropped pickups.

Exact match for bg_slidemove.c::PM_SlideMove. The retail body preserves the gravity branch, clip-plane loop, touch-entity accumulation, and triple-plane dead-stop handling from the shared slide-move core.

Retail PM_StepSlideMove entry point aligned with bg_slidemove.c::PM_StepSlideMove. The HLIL shows the classic one-argument boundary calling PM_SlideMove, attempting the step-up trace, and printing the stepped / bend can't step diagnostics before clipping velocity on landing; this differs from the current tree's extended step-height signature.

Exact match for bg_pmove.c::PM_CheckWaterJump. The retail body checks for the two-deep water case, traces 30 units forward and then 16 units up, and on success seeds the 200/350 takeoff velocity plus the water-jump timer.

Exact match for bg_pmove.c::PM_WaterJumpMove. It advances the movement through PM_StepSlideMove with gravity, subtracts gravity from vertical velocity, and clears the water-jump timer as soon as the player starts falling.

Exact match for bg_pmove.c::PM_WaterMove. The retail flow begins with PM_CheckWaterJump, otherwise applies PM_Friction, builds the 3D command vector through PM_BuildWishMove3D, falls back to the idle -60 sink vector, clamps wishspeed by the swim scale, accelerates with PM_Accelerate and pm_wateraccelerate, clips against the ground plane when needed, and finishes with PM_SlideMove(qfalse).

Exact match for bg_pmove.c::PM_FlyMove as reached by spectator and flight-powerup dispatch. It applies PM_Friction, builds the full 3D wish vector through PM_BuildWishMove3D, accelerates with PM_Accelerate and pm_flyaccelerate, and then steps through PM_StepSlideMove(qfalse).

Exact invulnerability-holdable movement helper from bg_pmove.c. Retail zeroes the command and velocity vectors, applies pitch-driven float or sink while the shield is held or decaying, decrements `playerItemTime`, clears the holdable slot when the timer expires, and forces the holdable legs-animation bits.

functions.signature: static void PM_AirControl(vec3_t wishdir, float wishspeed)
functions.comment: Exact air-control redirect helper from bg_pmove.c::PM_AirControl. Retail zeroes the vertical component, computes the planar dot with the wish direction, applies the classic `32 * pm_aircontrol * dot^2 * frametime` correction, renormalizes, and then restores the saved vertical speed.

Exact match for bg_pmove.c::PM_AirMove. The dispatcher reaches it only in the free-air path, and the retail body preserves friction, 2D wishdir construction, the air-control acceleration mode split for airstop versus strafe movement, the pm_wishspeed clamp on pure side movement, PM_Accelerate as the generic velocity pusher, optional PM_AirControl steering, ground-plane clipping, gravity step-slide, PM_InvulnerabilityMove, and the trailing PMF_DOUBLE_JUMP probe.

Exact match for bg_pmove.c::PM_GrappleMove. It offsets the grapple point by the forward vector, pulls toward that anchor, clamps short pulls to a scaled length and long pulls to the grapple speed, then marks the player off the ground plane.

Exact ladder-movement path from bg_pmove.c::PM_LadderMove. Retail applies PM_Friction, builds a full-axis wish direction through PM_BuildWishMove3D, overrides explicit climb/descent input to +/-0.66 * speed, caps total wishspeed to the same 66% limit, accelerates with PM_Accelerate and pm_accelerate, clips against the ground plane when needed, and finishes with PM_SlideMove(qfalse).

Exact ladder probe from bg_pmove.c::PM_CheckLadder. Retail normalizes the horizontal forward vector, traces one unit ahead with `MASK_PLAYERSOLID`, and latches the ladder flag when the contacted surface carries `SURF_LADDER`.

Exact match for bg_pmove.c::PM_WalkMove. The retail path begins with the water transition gate, the PM_ShouldUseInvulnerabilityMove handoff through PM_StepSlideMove(qfalse) and PM_InvulnerabilityMove, and PM_CheckJump, then applies friction, refreshes the crouch-slide timer, projects forward/right onto the ground plane, applies duck/wade speed clamps including the crouch-slide 0.75 duck scale, splits slick/knockback acceleration, clips downhill motion, and resolves the final step move.
Exact match for bg_pmove.c::PM_DeadMove. When the player is walking on the ground it trims movement speed by 20 each frame and zeroes the velocity once the corpse falls below the remaining threshold.

Exact match for bg_pmove.c::PM_NoclipMove. The retail helper forces DEFAULT_VIEWHEIGHT, applies the 1.5x friction variant, rebuilds the full 3D command vector through PM_BuildWishMove3D, accelerates with PM_Accelerate and pm_accelerate, and integrates origin directly by frametime * velocity without collision tracing.

Exact match for bg_pmove.c::PM_FootstepForSurface. It first honors the pmove noFootsteps gate, then switches on the current ground-surface flags for no-steps, metal, snow, wood, or default footstep events.

Exact match for bg_pmove.c::PM_CrashLand. The retail body chooses the landing animation, solves the quadratic for impact velocity, scales damage by ducking and water depth, suppresses fall events below the gib-health gate, clears the PMF_DOUBLE_JUMP latch, emits the matching fall event, and resets bobCycle.

Exact match for bg_pmove.c::PM_CorrectAllSolid, anchored by the preserved allsolid debug line. The retail helper jitters the origin across the 3x3x3 probe cube, retraces 0.25 units down into pml.groundTrace on success, and otherwise clears groundEntityNum plus groundTraceLatestEntNum/Time/Normal before dropping pml.groundPlane and pml.walking.

Exact match for bg_pmove.c::PM_GroundTraceMissed, anchored by the preserved lift debug line. The retail body traces 64 units down when the floor falls away, chooses LEGS_JUMP or LEGS_JUMPB/PMF_BACKWARDS_JUMP from forwardmove, and then clears groundEntityNum, groundTraceLatestEntNum/Time/Normal, pml.groundPlane, and pml.walking.

Exact match for bg_pmove.c::PM_GroundTrace. The retail flow traces 0.25 units down, routes allsolid recovery through PM_CorrectAllSolid, handles lift misses, kickoff dot > 10, and steep normals by setting pml.groundPlane without walking, records groundTraceLatestEntNum/Time/Normal on valid ground, clears PMF_TIME_WATERJUMP/PMF_TIME_LAND timers on landing, calls PM_CrashLand, applies the -200 z-velocity PMF_TIME_LAND/250 ms latch, updates PM_RecordGroundContact on fresh or changed ground, and finishes through PM_AddTouchEnt.

Exact match for bg_pmove.c::PM_SetWaterLevel. It clears watertype and waterlevel, then samples MASK_WATER at MINS_Z + 1, halfway from MINS_Z to viewheight, and head height at viewheight to promote the player through shallow, mid, or fully submerged water levels.

Exact match for bg_pmove.c::PM_CheckDuck. The retail helper switches the mins/maxs and viewheight for invulnerability, death, ducking, and standing-up probes, then maintains crouchTime and crouchSlideTime inline with the recovered crouch-slide cleanup gates.

Exact match for bg_pmove.c::PM_Footsteps. It computes xyspeed, preserves the crouch-slide movement branch, keeps airborne invulnerability on LEGS_IDLECR and waterlevel > 1 on LEGS_SWIM without advancing bobCycle, resets idle bobCycle when xyspeed < 5, suppresses ducked footsteps, applies the retail axis-speed run gate, and emits footstep, EV_FOOTSPLASH, or EV_SWIM when the bob boundary crosses.

Exact match for bg_pmove.c::PM_WaterEvents. The retail helper compares previous/current water levels, emits foot touch/leave events, suppresses head-water under/clear events while PM_DEAD, and suppresses EV_WATER_CLEAR while invulnerability is active.

Exact match for bg_pmove.c::PM_BeginWeaponChange. The retail helper validates the requested weapon against the owned-weapon bitmask, refuses to interrupt another dropping transition, emits EV_CHANGE_WEAPON, and starts the drop timer; the HLIL also preserves the retail sentinel branch for the no-weapon slot.

Exact match for bg_pmove.c::PM_FinishWeaponChange. It resolves the requested weapon from the current command, falls back when the slot is invalid or unavailable, raises the new weapon, applies the raise timer, clears the retail +0xe0 chaingun spin accumulator now named STAT_CHAINGUN_SPINUP, and starts the torso raise animation.

Exact match for bg_pmove.c::PM_Weapon. The retail body covers respawn/spectator/health gates, holdable use, attack-lockout and PMF_WEAPON_RESET cleanup, weapon cooldown and sentinel switching, ready/raising/dropping states, out-of-ammo handling, ammo consumption, EV_FIRE_WEAPON emission, Scout/AmmoRegen/Haste refire scaling, and the WP_CHAINGUN +0xe0 spin accumulator now named STAT_CHAINGUN_SPINUP that doubles refire while below 1000 and drains on release.

Exact match for bg_pmove.c::PM_Animate. It inspects the gesture and voice-command buttons, starts the matching torso animation when the torso timer is idle, and emits the taunt event on the gesture path.
Exact match for bg_pmove.c::PM_DropTimers. The retail helper decrements pm_time, legsTimer, torsoTimer, and the crouch-slide timer while clearing the PMF_ALL_TIMES flags once the misc timer expires.

Exact match for bg_pmove.c::PM_UpdateViewAngles. It rejects intermission and dead-player updates while preserving the PM_FREEZE dead-player exception, then applies the delta angles while clamping pitch to the same +/-16000 short-angle range as the GPL implementation.

Exact match for bg_pmove.c::PmoveSingle. The retail dispatcher seeds the pm globals, clamps msec, updates view angles, mirrors weaponPrimary/fov/forwardmove/rightmove/upmove into playerState, honors PMF_NO_MOVE before movement, branches through noclip/spectator/water/air/walk/grapple movement, then finishes with timers, weapons, footsteps, and final animation events.
Exact match for bg_pmove.c::Pmove. It bounds the server-time delta, advances the command time in 66 ms slices, and repeatedly invokes PmoveSingle to cover the whole movement window.

Exact retained MSVC scalar-deleting destructor for `std::bad_alloc`. It restores the `std::bad_alloc` vftable, runs the base `std::exception` destructor, and conditionally frees the object when the compiler-supplied delete flag is set.

Retail-only access-tier resolver for the current connection context. It queries the connecting client's auth state through the engine, returns root access for `localhost`, and otherwise looks up the cached `ban`/`mod`/`admin` tier in the access tree before ClientConnect, session init, and reload_access callers consume the result.

High-confidence access-file loader anchored by the exact initializing access list, invalid admin access format, and loaded %i steam ids into the access list prints. The retail body opens g_accessFile, parses symbolic `steamid|ban`, `steamid|mod`, and `steamid|admin` lines, skips comments, normalizes CRLF, and repopulates the cached SteamID privilege table.

Retail-only console command handler reached directly from ConsoleCommand on `reload_access`. It reruns G_LoadAdminAccessFile and then refreshes each connected client's cached privilege tier from the rebuilt access list.

Retail-only access-list dumper anchored by the exact `Access List: Page %i of %i` header. It paginates the cached SteamID access tree, labels entries as `PERM` or `TEMP`, formats each row as `%llu %s %s`, and prints either to the requesting client or to the dedicated server console.

Retail-only access-file persistence helper called from game shutdown. It writes the preserved `/reload_access` guidance header and then serializes the permanent access entries back into g_accessFile as `steamid|ban`, `steamid|mod`, or `steamid|admin` lines.

Retail-only access-tree lookup/insert helper. Given a SteamID key, it returns the writable privilege slot for an existing entry or allocates a new node and returns the same tier field for loader, tempban, addmod, and addadmin callers to update.
Retail-only access-tree reset helper. It destroys any cached nodes, restores the sentinel root/first/last pointers, and zeros the tracked access-entry count before reload and shutdown paths continue.

Retail-only access-tree erase helper. It removes the iterator range returned by the matching SteamID lookup, rebalances the tree, updates the first/last sentinels, and decrements the cached access-entry count used by list and reload flows.
Descriptive internal access-tree node destructor. It recursively descends the SteamID-ordered admin-access tree and frees every non-sentinel node during reset and shutdown cleanup.

Descriptive retained shutdown leaf for the global admin-access cache. After the paired tree-clear path has already removed every cached admin-access node, this helper only frees the 0x28-byte sentinel header allocation.

functions.signature: qboolean G_AdminAccessKeyLess(const unsigned long long *lhs, const unsigned long long *rhs)
functions.comment: Descriptive internal SteamID ordering predicate for the cached admin-access tree. It compares the 64-bit key fields exactly the way the adjacent lookup and insert helpers expect.

Descriptive internal red-black-tree erase helper for the cached admin-access table. It unlinks the selected node, updates the cached first and last iterators, performs the standard rebalance rotations, and returns the successor iterator through the out slot.
Descriptive internal in-order successor helper for the cached admin-access tree. It advances an iterator node pointer to the next SteamID entry in sorted order.
Descriptive internal left-rotation helper for the cached admin-access red-black tree. It promotes the current node's right child and rewires the parent and sentinel-root links.
Descriptive internal right-rotation helper for the cached admin-access red-black tree. It promotes the current node's left child and rewires the parent and sentinel-root links.
Descriptive internal rightmost-node walk for the cached admin-access tree. The erase path uses it when it needs to rebuild the sentinel's cached last-entry pointer from a replacement subtree.

Descriptive internal hinted-insert helper for the cached admin-access tree. It checks the supplied iterator hint against the pending SteamID key and either links immediately or falls back to the general unique-insert walk.

Descriptive internal unique-insert helper for the cached admin-access tree. It walks the SteamID order, links the pending node on misses, or frees that pending node and returns the existing iterator on duplicates.

Descriptive internal link-and-rebalance helper for the cached admin-access tree. It attaches the supplied node as the requested child, updates the cached first and last iterators plus entry count, and performs the standard red-black-tree insert fixup.
Descriptive internal in-order predecessor helper for the cached admin-access tree. It supports both normal iterator decrement and the sentinel-end fallback used by the insert-hint path.
Descriptive internal iterator-advance helper for the cached admin-access tree. It walks a node pointer forward or backward by a signed count and is reused by the paged access-list printer.

Descriptive internal constructor for cached admin-access nodes. It allocates the 0x28-byte node, seeds the sentinel-linked tree pointers, and copies the SteamID plus privilege-mode payload fields from the source tuple.
Retail-only access-tree range finder. It walks the SteamID-ordered cache and returns the begin/end iterators for a matching entry so unban and demote can erase the correct access node.
End-of-frame damage accumulator that converts client damage totals into damagePitch/damageYaw, EV_PAIN, and the outgoing damageCount payload.
Water, drowning, lava, and slime handler anchored by the gurp sounds and the battlesuit/world-damage control flow.
Loop-sound selector for proxmine ticking and lava/slime fry ambience.
Unique touch-entity walker that dispatches bot self-touch callbacks and touched-entity handlers after pmove.
Trigger scanner using the 40/40/52 spectator-safe range box, ET_ITEM touch checks, and jump-pad frame reset logic.
Spectator pmove path that sets PM_SPECTATOR, unlinks before pmove, touches triggers afterward, and handles follow cycling from the attack-button edge exactly like the source spectator think routine.
Inactivity watchdog that refreshes timers on input, warns shortly before timeout, and drops idle clients with the retail inactivity strings.
Retail-only flood-drop helper reached from ClientThink_real; it decays floodCount/floodLastTime against the flood-protection cvars and drops clients who exceed the server flood limit.

Descriptive retail-only split from the current G_RunFactoryRegen helper. Tailcalled from ClientTimerActions, it advances the health regen accumulator after the last-damage delay expires, stops once health reaches the configured target, and applies whole-point health ticks to ent->health.

Descriptive retail-only split from the current G_RunFactoryRegen helper. Tailcalled from ClientTimerActions after the health sidecar, it advances the armor regen accumulator after the last-damage delay, honors the regenArmorAfterHealth gate, and applies whole-point armor ticks into ps.stats[STAT_ARMOR].
Once-per-second client maintenance that accumulates timeResidual, applies regen and armor-decay behavior, and emits the regeneration event through adjacent Quake Live subhelpers.

Server-side player event switch reached from ClientThink_real. It walks recent playerstate events, applies fall damage, fires weapons, and handles teleporter, medkit, kamikaze, portal, invulnerability, and Harvester skull-drop use-item side effects.

Static living-client overlap test reused by ClientThink_real during invulnerability expansion. It scans the active client entities and returns true once the expanded mins/maxs still intersect another live client.
Predictable-event flusher that turns pending playerstate events into temporary ET_EVENTS entities addressed to all other clients.
Main per-command client simulation path covering command-time clamping, spectator and inactivity branches, pmove, events, linking, impacts, respawn checks, and once-per-second timers.
HLIL-visible native export that fetches the latest usercmd, stamps lastCmdTime, and forwards non-bot clients into the deeper think routine.
Spectator follow/POI/scoreboard updater that mirrors followed players and falls back to free or scoreboard spectators when follow targets disappear.
Per-client end-of-frame maintenance covering spectator handoff, expired powerups, world effects, connection flagging, and playerstate-to-entitystate sync.

Descriptive retail-only shared Clan Arena / Attack and Defend respawn helper. Reached from the dead-client respawn gate once those modes push a player back into spectator follow, it copies the corpse into the body queue, forces PM_SPECTATOR, reruns ClientSpawn, and enters follow mode when both teams still have active players.

Descriptive retail-only Attack and Defend timeout helper. It returns true only when both sides still have active players, no objective winner was already latched, and the active-round elapsed time has reached `roundtimelimit * 1000`.

Descriptive retail-only Attack and Defend controller read helper. It resolves any expired deferred AD_RoundStateTransition work and returns the current A/D round-state value for damage, objective, and HUD callers.

Descriptive retail-only Attack and Defend scoreboard helper. It records the latest per-turn score delta into the 20-entry circular history, rebuilds the ordered history window, and republishes the scores_ad payload.

Descriptive retail-only team scoreboard serializer for GT_TEAM. It emits the scores_tdm payload with the current red and blue totals plus the expanded per-client team stat block used by the retail scoreboard UI.

Descriptive retail-only Clan Arena scoreboard serializer. It emits the scores_ca payload with the round-oriented per-client stat block that includes team, score, accuracy, and arena-specific combat counters.

Descriptive retail-only shared CTF-style postgame stats publisher. During intermission it walks connected clients and emits the ctfstats payload with the retained capture, defense, return, and related objective aggregates used by the retail team-mode scoreboard UI.

Descriptive retail-only Red Rover scoreboard serializer. It emits the scores_rr payload with the team totals and the Red Rover per-client stat block used by the retail scoreboard and intermission UI.

Retail private voice-chat entry from the `vtell` seam. HLIL shows it validating the target slot, concatenating argv[2..], resolving both client names, and logging `vtell: %s to %s: %s\n`, which lines up with the GPL `Cmd_VoiceTell_f` ownership even though the visible retail body is trimmed back.
Exact retail `vtaunt` handler. HLIL preserves the same enemy-versus-lastkill checks, corpse and gauntlet taunt flow, and teammate reward scan used by g_cmds.c::Cmd_VoiceTaunt_f.
Exact team-order relay from g_cmds.c::Cmd_GameCommand_f. It parses the player and order indices and emits the selected `gc_orders[]` message through `G_Say` to both the target and the caller.
Exact location-print helper from g_cmds.c::Cmd_Where_f. HLIL shows it formatting the caller origin into the shared eight-slot `vtos` ring and sending the resulting `print "%s\n"` server command.

Descriptive retail-only callvote character-class helper immediately underneath `G_IsSafeVoteToken`. It accepts only alphanumeric and underscore bytes across the bounded token scan, while `G_IsSafeVoteToken` layers the semicolon rejection on top.

Descriptive retail-only callvote token validator. Used on the raw command and argument argv tokens before vote parsing, it rejects semicolons and accepts only alphanumeric or underscore characters through the adjacent character-class helper.

Descriptive retail-only `callvote kick` target predicate. HLIL shows it scanning connected clients for either a direct or sanitized netname match and returning success once the requested player name resolves.

Descriptive retail-only `callvote clientkick` validator. It returns true only when the supplied slot maps to a currently connected client, matching the active-slot gate used before the retail server-admin protection check.
Vote-start handler with the retail vote-limit enforcement and callvote-help strings.

Descriptive retail-only public-vote bootstrap helper. It promotes the pending caller, broadcasts `%s called a vote.\n`, seeds each connected client's public vote-state latch, marks the caller as the initial yes vote, and pushes the live vote configstrings.

Friendly-fire complaint command handler identified by the exact complaint -1/-2/-3 flow, the warning and kick prints, and the retail prompt-response path that resolves the pending complaint for the current victim.
Descriptive retail-only nextmaps tally rebuild. HLIL shows it recounting the three client selection buckets and rebuilding the corresponding key/value payload that feeds the arena-vote UI.

Descriptive retail-only nextmaps ballot path reached from `Cmd_Vote_f` while the arena-vote UI is active. It validates the numeric selection against the `nextmaps` table, enforces the retail vote throttle, updates the caller latch, rebuilds tallies, disables the UI, and broadcasts `%s voted for %s.\n`.
Vote-cast handler using the retail no-vote, vote-cast, and disable-vote-ui payloads.

Retail-only hidden console command reached from ConsoleCommand on `dumpvars`. It prints a compact playerstate snapshot including `clientNum`, `pm_type`, `pm_flags`, `pm_time`, `eFlags`, and the current origin vector for the selected client.
Cheat-gated setviewpos command identified by the exact usage: setviewpos x y z yaw and cheats-disabled prints; the retail body mirrors g_cmds.c::Cmd_SetViewpos_f.
Descriptive retail-only flag-carrier drop command. HLIL shows the exact DropFlag disabled, non-flag-gametype, and pause-or-timeout rejection strings before it dispatches the shared flag-drop helper.
Retail drop-powerup command wrapper anchored by the exact DropPowerup disabled, non-team, pause/timeout, InstaGib, and gametype rejection prints before the projectile-style spawn path.
Retail rune-drop command wrapper anchored by the exact Runes are not enabled, DropRune is not available in %s, and pause/timeout rejection strings plus the matching rune-expiration spawn flow.

Retail weapon-drop command wrapper identified by the exact DropWeapon enabled, non-team, pause/timeout, InstaGib, and gametype rejection strings before it spawns the dropped weapon entity and updates the client inventory.
Thin retail forfeit-command gate that rejects pause/timeout and round-countdown cases locally, then defers to deeper forfeit eligibility and execution helpers.
Exact spectator-cycle command helper anchored by the `SPEC command error: Not enough arguments` diagnostic and the retail client-iteration path that resolves the next eligible spectator target.

Descriptive retail-only training ready-up helper reached from `Cmd_ReadyUp_f` on the local tutorial client. It restores the retail training cvar set, respawns each connected client through `ClientSpawn`, clears the stale ready/award flags, and sends the local `clearChat` / `clearSounds` commands before normal ready-up flow continues.

Ready-up command helper that toggles the retail per-client ready latch, enforces the minimum-player and team-presence gates, and prints the Ready/Not Ready centerprint broadcast while the match is still in warmup.
HLIL-visible client command dispatcher for chat, voice, follow/team, give/god/noclip/kill, vote, and callvote command families.

functions.signature: float G_DistanceToEntityBounds(const gentity_t *ent, const vec3_t point)
functions.comment: Descriptive retail-only distance helper reused by the splash-damage seam. It clamps a point against `ent->r.absmin` and `ent->r.absmax`, accumulates the per-axis gap outside the box, and returns the Euclidean distance from the point to the entity bounds.

functions.signature: static void G_AddDamagePlum(gentity_t *attacker, vec3_t origin, int damage, int mod)
functions.comment: Descriptive retail-only `EV_DAMAGEPLUM` temp-entity emitter paired with the recovered cgame damage-plum seam. HLIL shows it spawning the QL-only damage-plum event at the caller-staged origin, restricting delivery to the attacking client, storing the raw damage payload, and translating the incoming MOD into the staged retail weapon field consumed by cgame.

functions.signature: void ScorePlum(gentity_t *ent, vec3_t origin, int score)
functions.comment: Exact score-plum temp-entity helper from g_combat.c. The retail body spawns `EV_SCOREPLUM` at the supplied origin, restricts it to the scoring client with `SVF_SINGLECLIENT`, and stores both the client number and score payload in the event state.

Descriptive retail-only `EV_AWARD` temp-entity emitter behind the Quake Live medal taxonomy. It spawns the QL-only award event at the scoring client's origin, increments the per-award count slab for the requested award id, stages the recipient client number plus award count/id payload for cgame, and refreshes the player's reward timer.

functions.signature: static void G_GrantPlayerReward(gentity_t *ent, int persIndex, int eFlag)
functions.comment: Descriptive retail-only reward-state helper reused by gauntlet, excellent, objective, and round-award paths. The leaf increments the requested persistant reward counter, clears the competing `EF_AWARD_*` bits, sets the supplied award flag, and refreshes `rewardTime` for the medal sprite display.

functions.signature: void AddScore(gentity_t *ent, vec3_t origin, int score)
functions.comment: Exact scoring helper from g_combat.c. The retail body gates out warmup/intermission scoring, emits the score plum, applies the raw delta to `PERS_SCORE`, forwards GT_TEAM score changes into the team totals, and then recomputes ranks.

functions.signature: void TossClientItems(gentity_t *self, gentity_t *attacker, flagDropContext_t context, int meansOfDeath)
functions.comment: Exact death-drop helper from g_combat.c. The retail body iterates the carried powerup timers, drops non-team powerups plus the flag set through the standard dropped-item path, restores persisted flag metadata on the drops, and conditionally throws the active weapon when the player still owns ammo for it.

Harvester skull-drop helper matched by the carried-skull count in client persistant state, the exact `Red Skull` / `Blue Skull` item lookups, and the death-path call that spawns the dropped cubes before the corpse cleanup continues.

Retail gib helper matched by the exact kamikaze-timer owner scan, the `EV_GIB_PLAYER` event emission, the corpse invisibility/content clear, and the shared reuse from both the live-player gib path and the corpse-die wrapper.

functions.signature: void body_die(gentity_t *self, gentity_t *inflictor, gentity_t *attacker, int damage, int meansOfDeath)
functions.comment: Exact corpse-die wrapper from g_combat.c. The retail body ignores the extra damage callback arguments, checks the `GIB_HEALTH` threshold, and then tail-calls GibEntity.
Exact delayed kamikaze activation callback. The body immediately forwards into G_StartKamikaze and then frees the timer entity through the normal G_FreeEntity path.

Exact near-capture helper from g_combat.c. It keys off carried CTF/1FCTF flags, resolves the correct home-flag classname, skips dropped or hidden flag entities, and toggles the holyshit player-event when the carrier dies within 200 units of the capture point.

Exact Harvester near-score helper from g_combat.c. It checks the carried-skull count in `ps.generic1`, resolves the opposing obelisk, and toggles the holyshit player-event when the carrier dies within 200 units of the score point.

functions.signature: void player_die(gentity_t *self, gentity_t *inflictor, gentity_t *attacker, int damage, int meansOfDeath)
functions.comment: Primary death-path helper anchored by the exact Kill: %i %i %i log, <world>/<non-client>/<bad obituary> fallback strings, obituary temp-entity emission, reward handling, and the retail item-drop and corpse transition flow.

functions.signature: int RaySphereIntersections(vec3_t origin, float radius, vec3_t point, vec3_t dir, vec3_t intersections[2])
functions.comment: Exact geometric helper from g_combat.c. The body normalizes the incoming ray direction, solves the sphere intersection quadratic, writes one or two hit points through `VectorMA`, and returns the retail hit-count result.

functions.signature: qboolean G_InvulnerabilityEffect(gentity_t *targ, vec3_t dir, vec3_t point, vec3_t impactpoint, vec3_t bouncedir)
functions.comment: Exact invulnerability impact helper from g_combat.c. It reverses the incoming damage direction, intersects it against the target's 42-unit sphere, emits `EV_INVUL_IMPACT`, and optionally returns both the impact point and normalized bounce direction.

functions.signature: static float G_KnockbackScaleForMOD(int mod, qboolean selfInflicted)
functions.comment: Exact knockback-scale selector from g_combat.c::G_KnockbackScaleForMOD. HLIL preserves the MOD-to-weapon switch, the `g_knockback_%s` cvar lookup path, and the self-damage overrides for rocket and plasma knockback before the final float scale is returned to G_Damage.

functions.signature: int CheckArmor(gentity_t *ent, int damage, int dflags)
functions.comment: Exact armor-save helper from g_combat.c. The retail body rejects null clients plus `DAMAGE_NO_ARMOR`, applies the active armor-protection scale, clamps the saved amount to the remaining armor total, and subtracts the final save from `STAT_ARMOR`.

Descriptive pure friendly-fire severity classifier beneath `G_Damage`. Retail buckets one team-damage incident into four levels using `<25`, `<50`, `<75`, and `>=75` thresholds before packing that level into the per-client team-damage status field.

functions.signature: void G_Damage(gentity_t *targ, gentity_t *inflictor, gentity_t *attacker, vec3_t dir, vec3_t point, int damage, int dflags, int mod)
functions.comment: Main damage routine identified by the preserved g_knockback_cripple, g_knockback_pg_self, and g_knockback_rl_self cvar reads, the friendly-fire training warning, and the exact damage and armor debug prints used by the retail combat path.

Descriptive retail-only spectator-warning leaf reused by the splash/trace damage seam. It resolves the supplied entity slot to a client, checks the spectator session-state gate, and emits the preserved `A spectator has been shot!` print only for spectator hits; the current source keeps the same message inline under `G_Damage` rather than as a standalone helper.

Exact line-of-effect helper from g_combat.c. Retail traces from the supplied origin to the midpoint and the four +/-15 corner offsets of the target bounds, returning true on the first unobstructed hit.

functions.signature: qboolean G_RadiusDamage(vec3_t origin, gentity_t *attacker, float damage, float radius, gentity_t *ignore, int mod, qboolean *splashMidAir)
functions.comment: Exact retail splash-damage walker from g_combat.c. It expands an entities-in-box query from the radius, applies the edge-distance falloff, gates each candidate through CanDamage, updates splash-hit telemetry, and then fans into G_Damage.

functions.signature: qboolean G_RadiusDamageOffset(vec3_t offsetOrigin, gentity_t *attacker, float damage, float radius, gentity_t *ignore, int mod)
functions.comment: Descriptive retail-only alternate splash helper used by the offset-origin explosion path. It mirrors G_RadiusDamage, but preserves the separate offset point for distance and damage-direction calculations before falling back to the normal splash resolution.

functions.signature: qboolean Weapon_LightningDischargeDamage(vec3_t origin, gentity_t *attacker, float damage, float radius)
functions.comment: Exact lightning-water discharge splash helper from g_weapon.c. It scans the radius box, reuses CanDamage, and applies MOD_LIGHTNING_DISCHARGE damage to each valid client in the discharge volume.

Descriptive retail-only Domination reward fan-out used by the point capture controller. HLIL shows it iterating the captured point's participant list, comparing each client against the primary-capturer pointer stored on the point entity, then awarding either `CAPTURE` or `ASSIST` score/eFlag/reward-time credit before printing the shared reward message.

functions.signature: static void G_DominationSelectPrimaryCapturer(gentity_t *point, int firstCount, int secondCount, gentity_t **firstTeam, gentity_t **secondTeam)
functions.comment: Descriptive retail-only Domination owner-selection helper beneath the point capture controller. It chooses or stabilizes the point entity's primary-capturer pointer from the opposing participant lists, preferring the side that currently has uncontested presence while preserving an existing owner when that client is still present.

Descriptive retail-only delayed Domination point activation leaf queued by SP_team_dom_point. The helper traces down from the metadata entity origin, retries with the fallback probe when needed, snaps the settled position into both `s.origin` and `r.currentOrigin`, clears the local bounds, and then arms the point's live callbacks and first think time.

Descriptive retail-only Domination configstring updater. HLIL shows it recounting red-owned and blue-owned control points across the Domination metadata slab and publishing the totals through configstrings `0x2BC` and `0x2BD`.

Descriptive retail-only Domination defense-bonus helper reached from the team frag-reward path. It scans active control points owned by the attacker's team, requires the victim to be within the 500-unit and PVS protection envelope of one of those points, and then awards the preserved `DEFENSE` score, stat, eFlag, and reward-time credit.

Descriptive retail-only Freeze controller read helper. It resolves any expired deferred state transition via Freeze_RoundStateTransition and then returns the current Freeze round-state value used by HUD and end-frame callers.

Descriptive retail-only shared Freeze state mutator spanning the current GPL tree's separate freeze-apply and thaw helpers. The frozen path latches the client into the round's inert state and updates the round counts, while the thaw path clears the frozen state, emits the matching temp entities, and restores the client to active play.

Descriptive retail-only per-client Freeze reset helper. It is reused by both the spawn/bootstrap path and the delayed active-client reset path, restoring clients to PM_NORMAL during warmup and active round starts or pushing them back into the spectator-side holding state when the controller is not yet ready.

Descriptive retail-only hidden Freeze team predicate consumed by G_FreezeEvaluateRoundWinner. HLIL shows it scanning connected clients on a register-passed sessionTeam and returning true only when no members remain unfrozen, letting the outer winner helper distinguish win versus draw.

Descriptive retail-only Freeze result helper. It compares the PM_NORMAL living-player tallies and, when the configured draw delay expires, the corresponding living-health totals, then stores the winning team latch used by the adjacent round-state controller.

Descriptive retail-only Freeze exit-rule checker used by the round-state controller. It evaluates the timelimit, roundlimit, and mercylimit conditions for the active Freeze round, optionally broadcasting the corresponding `... hit.` message before returning whether the round should end.

Retail Freeze round-state controller anchored by the preserved invalid-state diagnostic. It resolves pending transition timers, updates the match-state configstring, resets clients for warmup and active states, and applies the freeze round-complete transition.

Source-faithful outer Freeze frame driver. Retail keeps the round-state readback and winner-selection work split into adjacent helpers, but this boundary still performs the per-frame freeze update and round-end dispatch.

Descriptive retail-only Freeze round helper. It services deferred Freeze_RoundStateTransition work, counts active players by team during the live round, and triggers the shared last-alive notification path when the queried side is down to one thawed survivor.

functions.signature: static gentity_t *G_FreezeFindThawHelperByClientNum(const int *clientNums, int clientCount, int helperClientNum)
functions.comment: Descriptive retail-only Freeze thaw-helper lookup beneath `G_FreezeClientEndFrame`. It walks the supplied helper-client list, resolves each live client entity, and returns the first helper whose clientNum matches the requested thaw-progress target.

Source-faithful frozen-client end-frame handler. It tracks thaw progress, scans nearby allies with line-of-sight and distance gates, applies auto-thaw timing, and eventually fans into the shared retail Freeze state mutator when the client should thaw.
Descriptive gameplay MOD classifier used by the kill/death bookkeeping seam. HLIL shows it returning true only for `MOD_CRUSH`, `MOD_TELEFRAG`, and the retail Freeze thaw kill mode `MOD_THAW`.

Descriptive retail-only Quad Hog touch wrapper. It resolves the touching client's netname, broadcasts the `"%s is the ^5Quad Hog!"` centerprint, and then falls through to the shared `Touch_Item` pickup path.

Descriptive retail-only Quad Hog item launcher. It allocates a dropped `item_quad` entity, installs the custom Quad Hog touch wrapper, enables dropped-item bounce physics, sets the standard 30-second free timer, and links the entity into the world.

Descriptive retail-only Quad Hog world-item spawner. It chooses a random `info_player_deathmatch` origin, constructs the special Quad item entity there, plays the powerup-respawn sound, and broadcasts the `^5Quad ^7respawned!` centerprint.

Descriptive retail-only Quad Hog maintenance helper. It returns early while any live player is carrying Quad, scans the world for an active `item_quad` entity, and respawns one through `G_SpawnQuadHogQuad` when the mode is enabled in FFA and no world Quad remains.

Descriptive retail-only key reset helper. It resolves the silver, gold, or master key classname from the carried bit, frees any dropped copies, and respawns the matching world key entity before the caller clears the carrier's key mask.

Descriptive retail-only dynamic persistant-powerup spawn helper. HLIL shows it choosing an eligible red, blue, or deathmatch spawn point, excluding the other active powerup spots, resolving the target item through BG_FindItemForPowerup, and constructing a live ET_ITEM entity on that spawn.

Descriptive retail-only level-init driver for the random persistant-powerup set. It gates on the spawnItemPowerup toggle, selects team-spawn alternation when both CTF sides have enough spots, falls back to deathmatch spawns otherwise, and seeds the Guard, Doubler, Armor Regen, and Invulnerability entries.

Descriptive retail-only dropped-rune respawn helper. It frees the expired persistant-powerup entity, selects a fresh preferred team spawn set when available, and reuses G_SpawnItemPowerup to reinsert that powerup into the level.

functions.signature: void Add_Ammo(gentity_t *ent, int weapon, int count)
functions.comment: Exact ammo grant helper from g_items.c. Retail adds the requested ammo into `ps.ammo[weapon]`, applies the Haste-era special cvar path for prox ammo, and clamps the result against the current starting-ammo ceiling.
Exact holdable pickup helper from g_items.c. It stores the holdable item index in `STAT_HOLDABLE_ITEM`, preserves the Kamikaze eflag side effect, and returns the stock 60-second holdable respawn.

Exact powerup pickup helper from g_items.c. Retail rounds timers onto second boundaries, applies the flight refill path, updates the per-powerup hold and pickup stats, fires Quad-specific side effects, and scans nearby opponents for the denied-reward event.

Exact persistent-powerup pickup helper from g_items.c. It stores the item index in `STAT_PERSISTANT_POWERUP`, remembers the entity in `client->persistantPowerup`, sets the matching powerup timer to `INT_MAX`, and returns `-1` to suppress normal respawn scheduling.
Exact retail ammo pickup helper. It forwards the item tag and resolved pickup quantity into `Add_Ammo` and returns the current ammo respawn delay.

Exact armor pickup helper from g_items.c. Retail branches on the armor tier, updates the corresponding pickup counters and timing fields, clamps the player's armor against the current cap logic, and returns the stock 25-second armor respawn.

Exact health pickup helper from g_items.c. It applies the small and mega-health cap rules, updates the retail health pickup telemetry, and returns the appropriate 35-second or megahealth respawn delay.

Exact weapon pickup helper from g_items.c, anchored by `Pickup_Weapon: item has infinite ammo`. Retail grants the weapon bit, applies the dropped-versus-map ammo split, preserves the grappling-hook infinite-ammo path, and returns the team or duel weapon respawn cvar.

functions.signature: static void G_SendSpectatorItemStateToClient(int clientNum, gentity_t *itemEnt, qboolean initialSync)
functions.comment: Descriptive retail-only spectator-overlay event builder beneath the major-item timer path. It validates the requested spectator recipient and tracked item entity, spawns the synthetic `QL_EV_ITEM_PICKUP_SPEC` temp entity, and fills the player, item, remaining-time, palette, and layout fields consumed by cgame's spectator pickup cache.

Descriptive retail-only spectator-overlay broadcaster. It verifies that the supplied entity is one of the tracked major-item classes and then fans the synthetic `QL_EV_ITEM_PICKUP_SPEC` state event out to every active spectator client when the overlay path is enabled.

Descriptive retail-only spectator-overlay resync helper. When a client enters the spectator observer path, it walks the live major-item set and pushes the current synthetic item-timer state for each tracked item back to that client.

Source-faithful item respawn helper anchored by `RespawnItem: bad teammaster`. It validates grouped item respawns through the teammaster/teamchain path, restores contents and visibility, emits the item respawn event, and selects the retail powerup or kamikaze respawn sound when appropriate.

Source-faithful item touch dispatcher anchored by `Item: %i %s\n`. It validates the touching client, routes by `item->giType` into the deeper pickup helpers, applies the pickup sound and event path, and updates the retail per-item pickup telemetry fields.

Exact dropped-item constructor from g_items.c. Retail spawns an `ET_ITEM`, sets `modelindex2 = 1`, installs `Touch_Item`, seeds gravity motion from the supplied velocity, routes dropped powerups through `G_DroppedPowerupRunFrame`, and chooses the flag-return, persistant-powerup respawn, or auto-free think path.
Exact retail item-drop helper. It builds the forward toss velocity from the owner's view angles, preserves the Attack and Defend quad-drop special case, and then tailcalls `LaunchItem`.

Descriptive retail-only damaged-health drop helper. When the dropDamagedHealth toggle is enabled, it fans the lost health out into 25- and 5-point item spawns around the damaged client using the shared LaunchItem path.
HLIL-visible retail trampoline that preserves the classic item-use respawn boundary by tailcalling `RespawnItem(ent)`.

Source-faithful team-objective validator anchored by the missing red/blue/neutral flag and obelisk warning strings. It verifies the required team items exist for the active gametype before the map can support that ruleset cleanly.

Source-faithful retail item-registration reset helper. It clears the `itemRegistered` table, restores the baseline always-precached items, and preserves the Quake Live conditional registration of Harvester skulls plus the factory-enabled persistant-powerup and extra health pickups.
Exact item-registration helper from g_items.c, anchored by `RegisterItem: NULL`. It validates the `gitem_t *` and marks the corresponding `itemRegistered` slot for later configstring publication.
Exact shared flag-carrier predicate from `bg_misc.c`. The helper returns true as soon as any of `PW_REDFLAG`, `PW_BLUEFLAG`, or `PW_NEUTRALFLAG` is active in the leading `playerState_t` slab.

Descriptive retail-only gclient powerup predicate. The helper is a null-safe wrapper around `client->ps.powerups[PW_QUAD]`, and the kill-path carrier announcer uses it to choose the `Quad Damage` label.

Descriptive retail-only gclient powerup predicate. The helper is a null-safe wrapper around `client->ps.powerups[PW_BATTLESUIT]`, and the adjacent carrier-announcement slab uses it to choose the `Battle Suit` label.

Descriptive retail-only gclient powerup predicate. The helper is a null-safe wrapper around `client->ps.powerups[PW_REGEN]`, and the carrier-announcement path uses it to choose the `Regeneration` label.
Descriptive retail-only gclient powerup predicate. The helper is a null-safe wrapper around `client->ps.powerups[PW_HASTE]`, and the carrier-announcement path uses it to choose the `Haste` label.

Descriptive retail-only gclient powerup predicate. The helper is a null-safe wrapper around `client->ps.powerups[PW_INVIS]`, and the carrier-announcement path uses it to choose the `Invisibility` label.

Source-faithful late item bootstrap anchored by `FinishSpawningItem: %s startsolid at %s`. It sets the ET_ITEM state, model indices, touch and use callbacks, the drop-to-floor path, and the delayed powerup spawn gate before linking the entity.

Source-faithful outer item spawn helper. It stores the `gitem_t`, parses the retail `wait` and `random` overrides, registers the item assets, seeds the persistent-powerup and global-sound flags, and schedules `FinishSpawningItem`.

Exact match for g_items.c::G_BounceItem. The retail body reflects the item trajectory delta against the trace plane, scales it by `physicsBounce`, and either settles the item or restarts the trajectory from the bounced position.

Retail dropped-item runner. It services `G_RunThink`, advances gravity trajectories, traces with the entity clipmask or `MASK_PLAYERSOLID`, frees nodrop items, and falls back to `G_BounceItem` on impact.
Retail dropped-powerup trigger bridge anchored by the trigger_push, trigger_teleport, and Couldn't find teleporter destination strings; the body matches g_items.c::G_DroppedPowerupRunFrame.

Source-faithful retail persistent-powerup toss helper from g_combat.c. Unlike the current simplified source version, retail reconstructs a dropped item spawn around the client, preserves the unobstructed placement trace, and arms the dropped entity through the item runframe path.

Descriptive retail-only initializer for the hidden `g_lagHaxHistory` rewind ring. It allocates `g_lagHaxHistory.integer` 0x30-byte history nodes per client through `G_Alloc`, stitches each slab into a circular doubly linked list, and seeds the current head table used by the rewind helpers.

Exact history recorder from the hidden retail lag-hax seam. Tailcalled from `ClientEndFrame`, it advances the per-client history ring, zeros the slot for non-player entity states, and otherwise stores `s.pos.trBase`, `r.mins`, `r.maxs`, and `level.time` for later rewind interpolation.
