Exact single-client rewind helper from the hidden retail lag-hax seam. It walks backward through the per-client history ring, unlinks the entity, interpolates `r.currentOrigin` toward the requested command time, copies the matching bounds from the newer history node, and relinks the rewound client.

Exact retail hitscan rewind wrapper. For eligible non-bot shooters it clamps the requested rewind time by `g_lagHaxMs`, saves each other live client's current origin and bounds into the flat restore slab, and then calls `G_TimeShiftClient` across the active client set.

Exact retail restore pass paired with `G_TimeShiftAllClients`. It walks the saved client slab, restores any slot stamped with the current `level.time` back into `r.currentOrigin`, `r.mins`, and `r.maxs`, and relinks each restored client.

Descriptive retained cvar callback for `g_inactivityWarning`. It resets the live per-client inactivity warning latch through `G_ResetClientInactivityWarnings`, then emits the standard tracked-cvar change broadcast when the server is configured to surface those updates.

Descriptive retained cvar callback for `g_instaGib`. HLIL shows it refreshing the live client ammo slab through the adjacent instagib/infinite-ammo sync helper before optionally broadcasting the normal `Server: ... changed to ...` line.

Descriptive retained callback shared by `g_playerheadScale` and `g_playerModelScale`. It walks connected clients and reruns `ClientUserinfoChanged` so the forced-appearance userinfo fields and downstream player-appearance config consumers pick up the new scale values immediately.

Descriptive retail-only native export helper used by the server snapshot builder for client entities. It allows forced client-entity visibility for same-team viewers, spectators, the active Red Rover infection phase, and the One Flag bot special case instead of relying only on the stock PVS test.

HLIL-visible native export helper that returns true only when both validated client slots are distinct, non-spectators, and not on the same team. This is stricter than a plain !OnSameTeam check because self and spectator pairs are rejected.

Descriptive retail-only native export helper used by the Steam voice relay path. It suppresses delivery to bots, muted senders, and self in non-tell modes; in open/all-talk cases it returns false, and otherwise it blocks relay unless the sender and recipient satisfy the same-team or spectator policy.

HLIL-visible native export classifier for live objective entities. It recognizes flag items in CTF and Attack/Defend, the One Flag flag carrier target, obelisk classnames in Overload, and the Quake Live extended item-objective flag path.

Descriptive retail-only Freeze native export helper for temp-entity event 0x58 from the thaw-progress path. It validates the Freeze gametype/controller state, confirms the temp entity and its linked player/entity, and returns true only for clients on the same team as the linked target.

HLIL-visible native export helper that returns true only when the validated client slot has sess.privilege == PRIV_ADMIN. The equality check is exact, so this is narrower than a generic non-zero privilege predicate.
HLIL-visible native export getter that validates the client slot and returns gclient->ps.persistant[PERS_SCORE] from offset 0x100.
Named native export that stores the import table pointer, returns the direct-export table, and reports native qagame API version 10.
Exact formatted print wrapper from g_main.c::G_Printf. It writes into a 1024-byte stack buffer with _vsnprintf and forwards the resulting text through the trap_Printf import.

functions.signature: void QDECL Com_Error(int level, const char *error, ...)
functions.comment: Shared fatal/drop error wrapper used by q_shared and bg_ helpers such as Q_strncpyz and Com_sprintf. The body formats the varargs message into a stack buffer, then forwards it to the trap_Error import.
Exact game-module fatal error wrapper from g_main.c::G_Error. Game-side callers use it for spawn, mover, and entity hard-stop diagnostics before the trap_Error import fires.
Entity-team linker that chains grouped entities and prints the grouped-entity summary.

Descriptive retail-only cvar-table helper reused by both G_RegisterCvars and G_UpdateCvars. It recognizes custom-setting CVars such as `weapon_reload_gauntlet`, `g_damage_sg_outer`, and the other weapon/factory override knobs, then sets or clears the corresponding bits in the published g_customSettings mask.
Initial game cvar-table registration pass that tracks custom settings, clamps gametype, and explicitly registers g_version.

Descriptive retail-only G_InitGame sidecar called immediately after G_RegisterCvars. It seeds the published configstring slab for factory metadata, Domination capture time, Red Rover survivor ping cadence, custom settings, and loadout state while snapshotting the related cvar mirrors consumed by later gameplay helpers.
Main cvar update pass that tracks modification counts, refreshes custom settings state, and fans out follow-on handlers.
Intermission spawn locator anchored by the info_player_intermission classname, the warning fallback, and the target-facing angle adjustment when an intermission target is present.

Descriptive retail-only helper that clears the cached spawn counters, then scans all entities for info_player_deathmatch, team_CTF_redspawn, and team_CTF_bluespawn classnames before G_InitGame continues.

functions.signature: void G_InitGame(int levelTime, int randomSeed, int restart)
functions.comment: Main level bootstrap. Retail inlines the world-session check and outer spawn loop inside this boundary.
Shutdown path that logs the shutdown banner and persists session state.
Descriptive retail-only helper factored out of the duel queue path; it returns the oldest waiting spectator who is not a scoreboard spectator or invalid follow target.

Duel queue promotion helper that counts active non-spectators, selects the oldest waiting spectator, calls SetTeam with the retail "f" payload, and resets warmup and game-state latches when a second player is added.
Duel queue demotion helper that picks level.sortedClients[1] when exactly two players are active, then calls SetTeam with the retail "s" payload to move the loser back to spectator.
Tournament intermission helper that increments the winner's wins and loser's losses using level.sortedClients, then refreshes both client configstrings through ClientUserinfoChanged.
Retail-only award publisher tailcalled from both SendScoreboardMessageToAllClients and CalculateRanks; it selects winning clients for the legacy award configstrings at 0x2B4, 0x2B5, and 0x2B8-0x2BB.
Scoreboard broadcast wrapper that loops connected clients through DeathmatchScoreboardMessage, then falls through into the shared retail award-configstring refresh at 0x100559E0.

functions.signature: int QDECL SortRanks(const void *a, const void *b)
functions.comment: qsort comparator for the scoreboard ordering that keeps scoreboard/follow spectators last and otherwise sorts active clients by session, score, and race-specific tiebreak state.

functions.signature: void G_CountAndSortConnectedClients(int *numNonSpectatorClients, int *numConnectedClients, int *follow1, int *follow2, int *numPlayingClients, int *sortedClients)
functions.comment: Descriptive retail-only client-list builder split out of the round-results controller. It counts connected, non-spectator, and actively playing clients, seeds the first two follow targets, writes the discovered client numbers into the caller array, and qsorts that list through the adjacent team/score comparator.

Main rank rebuild that counts connected and non-spectator clients, sorts level.sortedClients, assigns rank/tie state, updates score and leader configstrings, resends scoreboards during intermission, and then tails into the shared retail award-configstring refresh.
Per-client intermission transition that breaks follow mode, moves the client to the stored intermission origin/angles, switches to PM_INTERMISSION, and clears transient combat state.

Retail intermission bootstrap that latches intermission time, runs tournament score adjustment, moves every client to intermission, clears vote state, and performs the extended nextmaps/setup work around the stock GPL path.

Intermission exit handler that triggers match-end autoactions, removes the duel loser on tournament restarts, resolves the nextmaps rotation or map_restart path, writes session data, and pushes connected clients back to CON_CONNECTING.
Timestamped logfile writer used by init, shutdown, connect, disconnect, and match flow.
Intermission transition logger that prints exit, team-score, and per-client score lines while queueing the match shutdown flow.

Descriptive retail-only warmup gate used by `Cmd_CallVote_f`. The helper rebuilds the warmup-ready counts and bitmask, short-circuits once countdown warmup is already active, and otherwise returns true only when the current duel or team warmup state has enough ready players to enter the retail start path.
Intermission ready-mask and delayed-exit handler identified by the propagated ready bitmask, the nextmaps-dependent grace window, and the final ExitLevel transition.
Match-end tie detector that compares the active duel leaders or the red/blue team scores depending on gametype before exit-rule decisions.

Retail-only shared forfeit eligibility helper used by both the client command path and automatic match-flow checks, anchored by the forfeit rejection strings and the losing-team/player validation logic.

Retail forfeit executor that forces the losing side's visible score state into the forfeited marker, sets the match-forfeited latch, broadcasts the forfeiture message, and logs Players have forfeited.
Match-end rule checker anchored by the retail time, frag, and capture limit strings.

Retail-only duel queue helper that notices dirty pq updates, rewrites each eligible queued spectator's teamtask userinfo key to match the one-based queue slot, and republishes them through ClientUserinfoChanged.

Retail-only duel warmup helper keyed by g_warmupReadyDelay and g_warmupReadyDelayAction; it arms CS_READYUP_STATUS, then either forces players ready or moves unready players to spectate-only once the deadline expires.
Warmup and game-state transition logic used by the tournament flow checker.

Retail-only vote tally helper that walks per-client vote states, updates the yes/no configstrings, and short-circuits explicit player pass or veto outcomes before returning a status code to CheckVote.

Quake Live-specific vote executor for cointoss, random, loadouts, timers, shuffle, teamsize, and weaprespawn commands; returns false so CheckVote can fall back to the generic console-command path for ordinary vote strings.
Retail-only vote reset helper that clears vote timing/display configstrings, resets the extra Quake Live vote UI state configstring, and zeroes each client's vote-status field.
Outer vote result checker that executes delayed vote strings, handles pass/fail/timeout outcomes, and clears vote state; Quake Live adds the explicit vote-expired print path.
Password watcher that recomputes g_needpass when g_password changes and republishes the paired server configstrings.
Entity callback dispatcher that preserves the classic nextthink/ent->think gate, emits the NULL ent->think error, and also services the adjacent pre-think callback slot at ent+0x2FC.

Descriptive retail-only publisher for the auxiliary team-count configstrings at 0x297 and 0x298. It refreshes them at a 250 ms cadence, using raw TeamCount in ordinary team states and the PM_NORMAL-filtered active-player counter during round-controller states.
Top-level game frame loop advancing time, stepping entities, and running match state checks.

Exact missile runner from g_missile.c. The retail body handles guided-rocket steering, plasma/rocket/BFG acceleration updates, collision traces, sky cleanup, nail ricochet gating, impact dispatch, prox owner-exit logic, and the final `G_RunThink`.

functions.signature: gentity_t *G_SpawnConfiguredMissile(gentity_t *owner, vec3_t start, vec3_t dir, float speed)
functions.comment: Descriptive retail-only shared missile allocator beneath the grenade, rocket, plasma, and BFG constructors. It spawns an `ET_MISSILE`, binds `G_ExplodeMissile` as the default think, stores the owner pointer and owner number, snaps the launch origin and delta to network-friendly integers, and seeds the prestep timestamp before the caller fills in the weapon-specific fields.
Exact grenade projectile constructor. It spawns the `grenade` missile with `EF_BOUNCE_HALF`, gravity movement, grenade damage fields, and the standard prestep timestamp.

Descriptive retail-only rocket acceleration callback used when the configured rocket acceleration factor differs from `1.0`. It scales the missile velocity by the stored rocket factor and rearms the next acceleration tick from the retail rocket interval.

Descriptive retail-only guided-rocket steering callback. It reads the owning player's current view angles, rebuilds the forward steering vector, rewrites the missile's snapped base and linear delta from the current position, and rearms the short guidance think cadence used by the retail rocket guidance path.
Retail rocket constructor. The body spawns the `rocket` missile, seeds direct and splash damage, enables optional guided-rocket think state, and applies the rocket acceleration settings.

Descriptive retail-only plasma acceleration callback. It scales the plasma bolt velocity by the stored plasma acceleration factor and rearms the next acceleration tick from the retail plasma interval.

Exact plasma projectile constructor. It spawns the `plasma` missile, seeds the plasma damage fields, launches the bolt linearly from the prestep timestamp, and stores the plasma acceleration configuration.
Descriptive retail-only BFG acceleration callback. It scales the BFG projectile velocity by the stored BFG acceleration factor and rearms the next acceleration tick from the retail BFG interval.

Exact BFG projectile constructor. The retail body spawns the `bfg` missile, applies the BFG damage and splash fields, launches it linearly from the prestep timestamp, and stores the BFG acceleration configuration.

Retail grapple projectile constructor. It spawns the `hook` missile, assigns `Weapon_HookFree` as the cleanup think, seeds the grappling-hook damage/method fields, launches it from the prestep timestamp, and latches the owner's active hook pointer.

Exact proximity-mine projectile constructor from g_missile.c. Retail normalizes the launch direction, spawns a "prox mine" missile with the 3-second arm timer and proximity-launcher damage fields, seeds s.generic1 from the owner team, and launches it at 700 units per second with the standard missile prestep timestamp.

Exact trace helper from g_mover.c. The retail body selects ent->clipmask or MASK_SOLID, traces from either client ps.origin or s.pos.trBase, and returns the blocking gentity when the trace starts solid.
Exact proxmine clearance probe from g_mover.c. Retail traces from `trBase + 0.125 * movedir` out to `trBase + 2 * movedir` and only accepts the position when the trace stays clear and unsolid.

functions.signature: qboolean G_TryPushingProxMine(gentity_t *check, gentity_t *pusher, vec3_t move, vec3_t amove)
functions.comment: Exact mover-side proxmine push helper. The retail body derives the inverse angular basis from `amove`, applies the translational and rotational displacement to the mine, and then validates the new placement through `G_CheckProxMinePosition` before relinking.

Exact mover-space point rotator from g_mover.c. Retail copies the incoming point into a temporary vector and then overwrites the point in place with the three row dot-products against the supplied 3x3 basis.

functions.signature: qboolean G_MoverPush(gentity_t *pusher, vec3_t move, vec3_t amove, gentity_t **obstacle)
functions.comment: Exact mover-team pusher from g_mover.c. Retail preserves the total-bounds entity sweep, proxmine handling, rider/entity push loop, rollback through the pushed stack, and the bobbing-or-damaging crush fallback when the move is blocked.

Exact mover-team runner from g_mover.c. It resets the pushed cursor, evaluates every teamchain part, calls `G_MoverPush`, rewinds trajectories on obstruction, dispatches the blocked callback, and otherwise fires reached handlers once linear-stop movers hit their endpoint.

functions.signature: void MatchTeam(gentity_t *teamLeader, int moverState, int time)
functions.comment: Exact team-wide mover-state propagator from g_mover.c. Retail walks the full teamchain, inlines the `SetMoverState` body for each member, rebuilds the stationary or linear-stop trajectory from pos1/pos2, evaluates the new trajectory into currentOrigin, and relinks every slave.
Exact binary-mover return helper. It pushes the whole team back into `MOVER_2TO1`, restores the mover loop sound, and emits the closing transition sound when present.

Exact arrival handler for binary movers. The retail body flips the mover into POS2 or POS1, emits the matching endpoint sound, schedules `ReturnToPos1` plus target use on the opening leg, closes areaportals on the return leg, and preserves the `Reached_BinaryMover: bad moverState` diagnostic.

Exact master-use dispatcher for doors, plats, and buttons. Retail climbs to the teammaster when invoked on a slave, preserves the partial-travel reverse logic, and keeps the keyed-door open-sound override that substitutes the silver or gold cue before otherwise following the stock mover-state flow.

Exact mover bootstrap from g_mover.c. Retail handles `model2`, parses `noise`, `light`, and `color`, binds `Use_BinaryMover` and `Reached_BinaryMover`, seeds the stationary pos1 state, and computes `trDelta` / `trDuration` from the pos1-to-pos2 travel vector.

Exact door crush handler. The retail body preserves the team-flag special case, pops and frees other non-clients, damages clients when the door has damage configured, and reverses non-crusher doors through `Use_BinaryMover`.

Exact spectator-door teleporter from g_mover.c. Retail chooses the nearest trigger face on the thinnest axis, builds the centered exit point ten units beyond that face, computes the facing angles from the exit direction, and teleports the spectator there.

Descriptive retail-only keyed-door trigger touch gate. It only reacts to clients, teleports spectators away from closed keyed doors through `Touch_DoorTriggerSpectator`, and requires the matching silver/master or gold/master key bit before forwarding into `Use_BinaryMover`.
Exact generic door-trigger touch callback. Retail uses the PM_SPECTATOR gate for the spectator teleport side path and otherwise opens non-opening parent movers through `Use_BinaryMover`.

Descriptive retail-only keyed-door trigger builder. It clears takedamage across the mover team, computes the aggregate bounds and thinnest expansion axis, spawns the `door_trigger` volume, installs `Touch_DoorTriggerKeyed`, and then replays `MatchTeam` for the teammaster state.

Exact generic door-trigger builder from g_mover.c. Retail marks the mover team shootable, computes the expanded aggregate trigger bounds, spawns the `door_trigger` volume with `Touch_DoorTrigger`, and finishes by replaying `MatchTeam` at the current mover state.
Exact tiny retail wrapper that simply reapplies `MatchTeam(ent, ent->moverState, level.time)`.

functions.signature: qboolean G_TryPushingEntity(gentity_t *check, gentity_t *pusher, vec3_t move, vec3_t amove)
functions.comment: Mover push helper anchored by the pushed_p > &pushed[MAX_GENTITIES] assert and the matching g_mover.c rotation/push bookkeeping used by moving platforms and doors.

Exact door spawn constructor from g_mover.c. Retail reads `startsound` and `endsound` spawnvars, computes movedir/pos1/pos2, routes keyed doors through the dedicated keyed-trigger builder, and otherwise preserves the health, touch-trigger, and team-chain initialization flow.

Exact player-list command from the retail direct command table. It walks connected clients, formats the same `%2d %llu %c %s` rows as the stock player listing, and routes them either to the console or the requesting client.

Descriptive retail-only direct-command state gate shared by `allready`, `abort`, and the timeout starters. The bitmask argument selectively permits warmup, round-countdown, or intermission usage; otherwise the helper emits the exact current-state rejection string and returns false.

Retail-only shared admin-command target resolver. It requires argv 1 to be a numeric PlayerID from the `players` list, validates the slot against connected clients, and emits the exact missing/invalid PlayerID diagnostics used by the direct admin command table.

functions.signature: int G_AdminParseTeamArg(int argvIndex, gentity_t *ent, int minTeam)
functions.comment: Descriptive retail-only direct-command team parser shared by `addteamscore`, `lock`, `unlock`, and `putteam`. It reads the requested argv slot, accepts the retail single-letter team tokens, enforces the caller-supplied minimum valid team, and prints the exact missing/invalid TeamName diagnostics on failure.

Retail-only direct admin command from the `abort` table entry. It blocks while a timeout is active, broadcasts either `%s has aborted the match` or `The server has aborted the match`, resets the match state to PRE_GAME, refreshes the timer configstring, and issues `map_restart 3`.

Retail-only direct admin command from the `addscore` table entry. It resolves a PlayerID target, parses the score delta from argv 2, adjusts the target's persistent score, announces `Player score adjusted.`, and broadcasts whether the player's score was raised or lowered.

Retail-only direct admin command from the `addteamscore` table entry. It parses a team token plus a numeric delta, adjusts that team's score, announces `Team score adjusted.`, and broadcasts whether the chosen team score was raised or lowered.
HLIL-visible admin ready helper from the allready command table entry; it validates access, enforces the duel two-player restriction, and sets every connected client's retail ready latch.

Descriptive retail-only team-lock guard reached from SetTeam. It permits spectator and free joins unconditionally, checks the per-team retail lock array for live red or blue joins, and otherwise prints `The %s team is locked!` to the console or requesting client.

Retail direct command-table handler for `lock`. It optionally parses a team token through G_AdminParseTeamArg, sets the matching retail team-lock latch or both live team locks when no team is specified, and broadcasts `The %s team is now locked`.

Retail direct command-table handler for `unlock`. It mirrors Cmd_Lock_f by clearing one parsed team-lock latch or both live team locks when no team argument is present, then broadcasts `The %s team is now unlocked`.

High-confidence team-move command from the direct admin table. It resolves argv 1 as a PlayerID, parses argv 2 through the shared team-token helper, preserves the same duel and countdown restrictions visible in the current source, notifies the target who moved them, and then forwards into SetTeam.

Descriptive retail-only shared timeout starter. After G_ValidateDirectCommandState approves the current match phase, it records the timeout owner and start/expire timestamps, refreshes the timeout configstrings, and broadcasts either `%s has paused the match` or `%s has called timeout (%ds)`.

Descriptive retail-only frame-side timeout-expiry handler. Called from G_RunFrame, it detects an expired timed timeout, shifts the affected client and entity timers forward by the paused duration, clears the active timeout state, and republishes the affected configstrings.

Retail direct command-table handler for `pause`. It starts an indefinite pause through G_StartTimeout when no timeout is active, and when the current pause is server-owned it lets a player caller take ownership of that timeout.

Retail direct command-table handler for `timeout`. Console use jumps straight into G_StartTimeout with the configured retail timeout length, while player use enforces the per-team timeout pool, updates the remaining-timeout configstrings, and then starts the timed timeout.

Retail direct command-table handler shared by the `timein` and `unpause` entries. It verifies caller ownership or privilege, arms the five-second resume countdown by rewriting the timeout-expire configstring, and announces either `The server has called timein` or `%s has called timein`.

Retail-only direct admin command from the `addadmin` table entry. It promotes the target player to admin privilege, writes the same tier into the cached access tree under the target SteamID, broadcasts `%s has become an administrator`, and pushes the updated `priv %i` payload to the target client.

Retail-only direct admin command from the `addmod` table entry. It promotes the target player to moderator privilege, mirrors that tier into the cached access tree, broadcasts `%s has become a moderator`, and sends the refreshed `priv %i` value to the target client.

Retail-only direct admin command from the `demote` table entry. It rejects same-or-higher privilege targets, clears the target's privilege tier, removes the matching SteamID from the cached access tree, broadcasts that the player's privilege was removed, and sends the new `priv %i` value to the target.

High-confidence admin command handler pinned by the retail command table entry for `mute` plus the exact `%s has been muted\n` broadcast. The body resolves a target client through the shared privilege-aware lookup helper, raises the target's muted session flag, and rejects same-or-higher privilege targets.

High-confidence admin command handler pinned by the retail `unmute` command-table entry plus the exact `%s has been unmuted\n` broadcast. It clears the target client's muted session flag after the same privilege-aware player lookup used by Cmd_Mute_f.

Descriptive retail-only shared direct-command target helper reused by the `kick` and `ban` command-table entries. It resolves the requested client through the same privilege-aware lookup used by adjacent admin commands, refuses admin targets with the exact `Can not kick admins.` print, distinguishes the ban path by comparing argv0 against `ban`, updates the target's access-entry mode, and finally drops the client with `was kicked`.

Retail-only direct admin command from the `unban` table entry. It parses argv 1 as a SteamID account number, locates the matching access-tree entry, erases it, and prints the exact `%llu has been unbanned` confirmation to either the console or the requesting client.
Retail-only broadcast chat command from the `opsay` table entry. It concatenates argv 1+, prefixes the message as `<@name>` or `<@server>`, and sends the formatted text to all connected clients.

Retail-only direct admin command from the `listaccess` table entry. It parses an optional 1-based page argument, converts it to the zero-based page index used internally, and forwards to G_PrintAccessListPage.

Retail-only direct command-table handler for `setmatchtime`. It parses argv[1] as a whole-second duration, rewrites the live match-time configstring at CS 0x0D from the current level time, and broadcasts the exact `pcp "Match time has been set to %s.\n"` confirmation.

Descriptive retail-only help printer for the direct command table. Reached from the `?` table entry, it walks `data_10080754`, filters commands by the caller's privilege tier and visibility flags, and prints the `command + description` help rows to either the console or the requesting client.

Descriptive retail-only direct command dispatcher. It scans the shared command table for a matching token, enforces the required privilege floor using either the caller's stored access tier or the console superuser tier, invokes the matched handler, and emits the exact insufficient-privileges diagnostic when access is denied.

Descriptive retail-only filename sanitizer shared by the auto-record helpers. It strips Quake color escapes, drops control and out-of-range characters, keeps only the retail-safe punctuation subset, caps the token at 39 bytes, and falls back to `invalid` when nothing survives.

Descriptive retail-only auto-record basename builder. In duel/team modes it sanitizes the live player or team names from the relevant configstrings, adds the optional `(POV)` marker for the local recorder, appends the map name when enabled, and finishes with the timestamp suffix used by the downstream `record` and `screenshot` commands.

Descriptive retail-only auto-record stop helper. When the armed record bit is set, it clears that latch and sends the exact `stoprecord` server command to every connected client currently flagged for per-client recording.

Descriptive retail-only late-join auto-record helper. If recording is already armed and the target client advertises the record-enabled bit, it rebuilds the shared basename and issues `record "%s"\n` for that one client.

Descriptive retail-only auto-record controller. It reacts to the live warmup and match-start latches by arming or clearing the record state, starts per-client recording once the match timer becomes active, and emits the delayed end-of-match `screenshot "%s"\n` captures after stopping active recording.

Standalone `functions.csv` body row carved out of `G_CheckAutoRecord` after the one-time sentinel initialization return. It contains the active record-start, delayed stop, and end-of-match screenshot path that the parent helper reuses once auto-recording is armed.

Descriptive retail-only timeout configstring refresher. It republishes the remaining red and blue timeout counts plus the active timeout start and expire timestamps through CS 0x29D-0x2A0 after timeout and match-state changes.

Descriptive retail-only race spawn/reset helper reused from both the race ClientBegin and respawn gametype switch cases. It clears the client's checkpoint-history buffer, resets the race-time sentinel, republishes CS_RACE_RECORDS, resends `race_init`, and then tailcalls ClientSpawn.

Retail race checkpoint touch handler anchored by the exact personal-best and finished-the-race prints, the point-to-point target-chain bookkeeping, and the matching split/final event emission used when a player advances or completes a run.

Descriptive retail-only `raceinit` client-command leaf. Its only preserved caller is the client command dispatcher, and the body itself clears `ent->client->raceState.active`, `startTime`, `nextCheckpoint`, and the in-progress split buffer so a player can restart a run without rebuilding the broader race metadata.

Retail race admin command handler identified by the combined clear, dump, and spawn flows plus the exact admin_race_point_%i and too many race points strings that match the current g_race.c command entry point.

Descriptive retail-only Red Rover controller read helper mirroring the Freeze-side resolver at 0x1004BC30. It advances any expired pending RR_RoundStateTransition and returns the current Red Rover round-state value.

Descriptive retail-only Red Rover spawn finalizer reached from ClientSpawn. Survivors fall through to the generic G_FinalizeSpawnLoadout path, while infected clients are forced onto the zombie role loadout by seeding the reduced weapon mask, selected weapon, and health/maxHealth directly.

Descriptive retail-only Red Rover infection damage helper. Called from the main damage path after armor resolution, it applies the shared self/team damage suppression policy, clamps survivor-vs-infected damage during the active infection state, accumulates per-attacker threshold credit, and converts each completed bucket into a score award plus CalculateRanks refresh.

Descriptive retail-only Red Rover per-client round reset helper. Reused from both ClientBegin and the delayed respawn path, it reruns ClientSpawn, reapplies the Red Rover role-aware spawn finalizer, and pushes the client into the holding state while the round controller is still in its pre-release phase.

Retail per-client Red Rover infection bootstrap reached from the spawn/begin path. It seeds the infection-role flag bits from sess.sessionTeam, clears those bits for survivors according to the current RR cvar toggles, and forces the holding-state flag while warmup or the pre-active round state is still in effect.

Descriptive retail-only Red Rover completion helper. It evaluates the connected-team counts gathered by the caller, applies the configured draw-delay gate before ending tied states, and records the last surviving infected client when the infection ruleset leaves exactly one zombie standing.

Descriptive retail-only Red Rover exit-rule helper. It suppresses match exit while ScoreIsTied is true, otherwise checks the RR timelimit and roundlimit paths, optionally broadcasts the corresponding print string, and logs the shared Timelimit hit./Roundlimit hit. messages.

Descriptive retail-only infection autojoin helper reached from SetTeam when Red Rover infection mode assigns a player without an explicit team request. It resolves any pending RR_RoundStateTransition work, then returns TEAM_RED for the preserved or newly selected infected slots and TEAM_BLUE for everyone else.

Descriptive retail-only infection round-seeding helper reached from the infection branch of G_RRTrackRoundActivity. It moves connected TEAM_RED participants back to TEAM_BLUE survivors, restores the latched infected client when one is already known, otherwise picks a random connected client from level.sortedClients, stores that slot, and returns the selected infected client number.

Retail Red Rover round-state controller anchored by the preserved invalid-state diagnostic. It resolves pending transition timers, updates the match-state configstrings, resets live participants, and drives the active, complete, and restart phases of the round flow.

Descriptive retail-only Red Rover infection helper. When the infection ruleset is active it checks the survival-bonus timer, awards the configured "Survival Bonus" score delta to the connected team-2 participants, emits the matching temp-entity event, and refreshes ranks.

Descriptive retail-only Red Rover infection helper. It sends the countdown centerprint for the next spread window and, once the delay expires, converts the next eligible connected participant through the deeper team/state mutation helper before rearming the timer.

Source-faithful outer Red Rover activity monitor. Called once per frame from the Red Rover branch in G_RunFrame, it refreshes the controller state, applies the survival-bonus and infection-spread helpers when infection is active, evaluates round completion, and advances the round-state controller when the round ends.

Descriptive retail-only Red Rover init helper reached from G_InitGame. It clears the infection-selection trackers, seeds the first pending controller state, and primes RR_RoundStateTransition for either the ordinary or infection-enabled opener.

functions.signature: void G_RRHandlePlayerDeath(team_t oldTeam, gentity_t *victim, int meansOfDeath)
functions.comment: Retail Red Rover death-path helper and closest analogue to g_client.c::G_RRHandlePlayerDeath. It receives the victim's pre-mutation team, flips or infects the victim, refreshes userinfo, emits the infection temp-entity when applicable, updates the infection timers/bonus state, and reevaluates round completion.

Exact session serializer from g_session.c::G_WriteClientSessionData. It formats the retail 14-field sess payload, including privilege, spectator queue state, skill slots, and muted state, and writes it to the per-client `session%i` cvar.

Exact reconnect/session restore helper from g_session.c::G_ReadSessionData. It reloads the `session%i` cvar, reparses the retail 14-field format, reapplies the non-maintained team fallback for team modes, and normalizes spectator-only state.

Exact first-connect session initializer from g_session.c::G_InitSessionData. It chooses the initial team from the current gametype, autojoin, and userinfo, seeds spectator defaults and admin privilege, handles tournament spectate-only joins, and persists the session immediately.

Exact world-session persistence helper from g_session.c::G_WriteSessionData. It stores the current gametype in the global `session` cvar and serializes each connected client's session block through G_WriteClientSessionData.

functions.signature: qboolean G_SpawnString(const char *key, const char *defaultString, char **out)
functions.comment: Exact spawnvar lookup helper from g_spawn.c::G_SpawnString. While spawning, it scans level.spawnVars for the requested key and otherwise falls back to the caller-supplied default string.

Exact classname dispatcher from g_spawn.c::G_CallSpawn. It validates ent->classname, checks the bg_itemlist item table first, then the static spawns[] table, and logs missing handlers when no spawn function exists.
Exact spawn-string allocator from g_spawn.c::G_NewString. It allocates a persistent copy through G_Alloc and converts escaped `\\n` sequences into literal linefeeds for multiline entity text.

functions.signature: void G_ParseField(const char *key, const char *value, gentity_t *ent)
functions.comment: Exact entity field parser from g_spawn.c::G_ParseField. It walks the retail fields[] table and decodes integer, float, string, vector, and anglehack spawn keys into the target gentity_t.

Exact spawnvar materializer from g_spawn.c::G_SpawnGEntityFromSpawnVars. It allocates an entity, parses all key/value pairs, applies the retail notfree/notteam/gametype/not_gametype rejection path plus class exemptions, copies origin into runtime position fields, and dispatches through G_CallSpawn or frees the entity.
Retail-only spawn helper that whitelists specific item and obelisk classnames against the normal notfree/notteam/gametype rejection path.
Brace-bounded entity parser. Retail inlines G_AddSpawnVarToken inside this boundary.
Worldspawn initializer that reads map metadata and sets up warmup and configstring state.
Direct ConsoleCommand target for `entitylist`. It walks the live entity slots, prints the same ET_* labels used by g_svcmds.c::Svcmd_EntityList_f, and appends the classname when present.

Exact match for g_svcmds.c::ClientForString. It accepts either a numeric client slot or an exact netname match and preserves the same `Bad client slot`, `Client %i is not connected`, and `User %s is not on the server` diagnostics.

Direct ConsoleCommand target for `forceteam`. It reads argv 1 through ClientForString, pulls argv 2 as the destination token, and then forwards straight into SetTeam exactly like g_svcmds.c::Svcmd_ForceTeam_f.

HLIL-visible console-command dispatcher for entitylist, forceteam, game_memory, addbot, botlist, game_crash, forceshuffle, dumpvars, and reload_access before the remaining hidden debug/admin fallthrough.

High-confidence retail shuffle implementation reached both from gameplay vote/admin paths and directly from ConsoleCommand on `forceshuffle`. The body prints `Shuffling Teams.\n`, gathers active players, sorts them through the shared balance comparator, redistributes teams, and forces the same respawn/reset flow exposed by the current shuffle command surface.

Descriptive Freeze-state predicate used across the retail Freeze controller seam. It returns true only when the active gametype is Freeze, the shared intermission latches are clear, and `level.warmupTime` has reached the live-round `0` state.

Descriptive Freeze-state predicate paired with `G_FreezeRoundActive`. It returns true only while Freeze is the active gametype, the shared intermission latches are clear, and `level.warmupTime` is still nonzero.
Exact team gesture broadcaster from g_team.c::Team_ForceGesture. It scans connected clients on the requested team and sets FL_FORCE_GESTURE on each entity.

Descriptive retail-only native helper that compares two gclient_t sessionTeam values directly. Unlike the stock GPL OnSameTeam path, the retail helper still allows matching spectator pairs when the gametype is below GT_TEAM.
Descriptive retail-only client-number wrapper around G_ClientsOnSameTeam. It validates both client slots against level.clients and then tails into the underlying sessionTeam comparison helper.

Entity-level same-team predicate used broadly across combat, chat, and objective logic. Retail keeps the classic client/sessionTeam equality test, but broadens the non-team-gametype case to treat matching spectators as same-team instead of always returning false.
Descriptive retail-only client predicate that validates the slot and returns true when the target client's sess.sessionTeam is TEAM_SPECTATOR.
Exact team-broadcast helper from g_main.c::PrintTeam. The retail body walks connected clients on the requested sessionTeam and forwards the prepared server command to each one.

Exact location resolver from g_team.c::Team_GetLocation. It scans the linked target_location chain, keeps the nearest in-PVS match, and returns the winning location entity for team overlays and voice reports.

Source-faithful team counter. The committed decomp still hides the register-passed ignoreClientNum, but HLIL preserves the classic ignore-self gate before counting connected clients on the requested sessionTeam.

Retail-only force-balance helper extracted around g_teamForceBalance. It returns true when the current red and blue roster spread is at most one player, and both SetTeam and Cmd_ReadyUp_f use it before applying stricter destination-team checks.
Warmup gate that checks the retail minimum-player thresholds for duel and team modes, including the stronger both-teams-present requirement used by readyup and allready.

functions.signature: void AddTeamScore(const vec3_t origin, int team, int score)
functions.comment: Exact global-team-score helper from g_team.c::AddTeamScore. The committed decompile obscures part of the calling convention, but the callsites and body still line up with the stock lead-change, tied-score, and scored sound/event selection before the team total increments.
Exact team-leader lookup from g_client.c::TeamLeader. The retail scan returns the first connected client on the requested team whose sess.teamLeader flag is set, otherwise `-1`.

Exact leadership assignment helper from g_main.c::SetLeader. It validates the target client, clears any existing leader on that team, republishes the affected userinfo, and prints the new-leader announcement.

Exact fallback leader selector from g_main.c::CheckTeamLeader. If no current leader exists for the team, the retail body first prefers the first non-bot teammate and then falls back to the first teammate regardless of bot status.

functions.signature: int QDECL SortClients(const void *a, const void *b)
functions.comment: Exact static comparator from g_team.c::SortClients. It sorts overlay client numbers in ascending order before TeamplayInfoMessage formats the `tinfo` payload.
Team overlay payload builder using the retail tinfo client-command format.

Exact periodic team-status updater from g_team.c::CheckTeamStatus. It refreshes each connected red and blue client's cached location via Team_GetLocation and then emits the current TeamplayInfoMessage overlay for those clients.

Exact flag-status publisher from g_team.c::Team_SetFlagStatus. It updates the stored red, blue, or neutral flag state, republishes CS_FLAGSTATUS with the One Flag remap when needed, and fans out the adjacent rankings flag-status event helper.

Exact team-message varargs helper from g_team.c::PrintMsg. It formats into the shared 1024-byte buffer, hard-stops on overrun, and sends the resulting print command either to the targeted client or to all clients.

Exact dropped-flag status helper from g_team.c::Team_CheckDroppedItem. It resolves the dropped team item tag and republishes the red, blue, or neutral flag state as dropped through Team_SetFlagStatus.
Team objective frag bonus helper backed by the exact flag/skull carrier frag messages, the Quad/Invis/Haste/Regen powerup strings, and the source-faithful g_team.c bonus path.

Descriptive retail-only CTF flag-sanity helper reached from the per-frame GT_CTF branch in G_RunFrame. It accepts the red or blue team, looks for any dropped or visible flag entity, falls back to a live enemy carrier with an active flag powerup, and only invokes Team_ReturnFlag when the flag has no surviving world or carrier representation.

Exact flag reset helper from g_team.c::Team_ResetFlag. It scans the matching flag classname for the requested team, frees dropped copies, respawns the base flag, clears dropped-flag state, and marks the flag status back at base.

Exact global return-sound helper from g_team.c::Team_ReturnFlagSound. It validates the passed flag entity, spawns EV_GLOBAL_TEAM_SOUND at the flag position, and selects the red or blue return eventParm.

Exact global pickup-sound helper from g_team.c::Team_TakeFlagSound. It throttles the red and blue taken timestamps, then emits the matching GTS_*_TAKEN EV_GLOBAL_TEAM_SOUND from the touched flag position.
Exact capture-sound helper from g_team.c::Team_CaptureFlagSound. It validates the passed flag entity, spawns EV_GLOBAL_TEAM_SOUND at the flag position, and selects the blue or red capture eventParm.

Exact return helper from g_team.c::Team_ReturnFlag. It resets the requested flag through Team_ResetFlag, emits the matching return sound, and prints either the neutral one-flag return line or the colored `%s flag has returned` announcement.
Exact dropped-flag cleanup helper from g_team.c::Team_FreeEntity. It inspects the flag powerup tag on the item entity and forwards into Team_ReturnFlag for red, blue, or neutral flags.

Compact retail dropped-flag think used as the timeout callback for flag drops. It resolves the dropped flag's team from giTag, resets the corresponding base flag, and emits the matching return sound without the newer neutral-flag ping branch present in the current source.
Home-flag touch handler anchored by the exact returned the %s flag and TEAM CAPTURED the flag prints plus the assist/capture award side effects from g_team.c::Team_TouchOurFlag.

Exact enemy-flag touch handler from g_team.c::Team_TouchEnemyFlag. It applies the Attack and Defend active-phase gate, updates the carried-flag powerup and status configstring, awards the pickup bonus, starts the hold timer, and plays the pickup sound.

Exact team-item pickup dispatcher from g_team.c::Pickup_Team. It handles obelisk and harvester team items inline, resolves red, blue, or neutral flag ownership from the classname, and routes CTF, One Flag, and Attack and Defend touches into Team_TouchOurFlag or Team_TouchEnemyFlag with the retail team gating.

functions.signature: void SpawnObelisk(vec3_t origin, int team, int spawnflags)
functions.comment: Overload and obelisk spawn helper anchored by the startsolid diagnostic.

Exact Overload obelisk-attack gate from g_team.c. The body verifies the target still uses `ObeliskDie`, rejects non-client attackers, returns early for same-team touches, and throttles the matching red or blue under-attack `EV_GLOBAL_TEAM_SOUND` announcement before allowing damage.

Descriptive retail-only Freeze tally helper. It sums entity health for the same connected PM_NORMAL clients counted by the adjacent active-player helper, and feeds the freeze round-end health summary and tiebreak path.

Descriptive retail-only shared team counter. HLIL shows it walking connected clients with ps.pm_type == PM_NORMAL and accumulating per-sessionTeam tallies for Freeze, Red Rover, teamsize checks, and the auxiliary team-count configstring publisher.
Descriptive retail-only roster counter that tallies every connected client by sessionTeam without the PM_NORMAL filter used by the adjacent active-player helper.

Descriptive retail-only shared round alert helper. It locates the sole surviving PM_NORMAL client on the selected team, emits the paired temp-entity/event, and sends that client the preserved centerprint notification used by the A/D, CA, Freeze, and Red Rover last-alive paths.
Exact trigger bootstrap from g_trigger.c. It applies `G_SetMovedir` when `s.angles` is nonzero, calls `trap_SetBrushModel`, and then restores the standard trigger contents and no-client server flags.
Retail gauntlet fire body. It consumes the target latched by `CheckGauntletAttack`, emits the melee hit effect, and applies quad-scaled `MOD_GAUNTLET` damage.

Exact match for the 43-unit gauntlet prefire probe used from `ClientThink_real`. It traces from the muzzle, rejects `SURF_NOIMPACT` and nondamage targets, and latches the struck entity for `Weapon_Gauntlet`.

functions.signature: void Bullet_Fire(gentity_t *ent, float spread, int damage, meansOfDeath_t mod)
functions.comment: Exact match for the generic hitscan bullet path. The retail body builds spread from random sin/cos offsets, emits wall or flesh impact temp entities, handles invulnerability bounce, and applies `G_Damage` with the supplied MOD.

functions.signature: qboolean ShotgunPellet(vec3_t start, vec3_t end, gentity_t *ent)
functions.comment: Retail single-pellet tracer. The body probes `MASK_SHOT`, uses the `g_damage_sg` or `g_damage_sg_outer` damage path, supports invulnerability ricochet, and returns `qtrue` only when `LogAccuracyHit` succeeds.

functions.signature: void ShotgunPattern(vec3_t origin, vec3_t origin2, int seed, gentity_t *ent)
functions.comment: Exact match for the shotgun spread loop. It derives forward/right/up from `origin2`, pulls offsets through `G_GetShotgunPelletOffsets`, and awards a single accuracy hit on the first successful pellet.
Retail shotgun event wrapper. It emits `EV_SHOTGUN`, seeds the pellet pattern, and delegates the actual spread loop to `ShotgunPattern`.
Retail grenade-launcher wrapper. It adds the upward pitch bias to `forward`, renormalizes the aim vector, calls the grenade projectile factory, and applies the quad damage multipliers.
Retail rocket-launcher wrapper around the rocket projectile factory with quad-scaled direct and splash damage.
Retail plasma wrapper around the plasma projectile factory with quad-scaled direct and splash damage.
Retail BFG wrapper around the BFG projectile factory with quad-scaled direct and splash damage.

Exact match for g_weapon.c::weapon_railgun_fire. The retail body preserves the unlink-trace-relink loop, invulnerability bounce handling, G_Damage application, temp entity trail emission, and IMPRESSIVE award bookkeeping.
Retail grapple-fire gate. It only spawns a new hook when `fireHeld` is clear and no hook is active, then latches `fireHeld` for subsequent frames.
Exact match for the grapple cleanup helper. It clears the parent client's hook pointer, removes `PMF_GRAPPLE_PULL`, and frees the hook entity.
Retail grapple think routine. It keeps the hook snapped to the latched enemy center when attached and mirrors the resolved point into the owner's `ps.grapplePoint`.
Retail lightning-gun beam path, including discharge checks for hazardous media, `EV_LIGHTNINGBOLT` temp entities, invulnerability bounce, and accuracy bookkeeping.
Retail nailgun wrapper. It loops `NUM_NAILSHOTS` times through the nail projectile factory and scales each missile's direct and splash damage by the quad factor.
Retail prox-launcher wrapper. It adds the upward pitch bias, renormalizes `forward`, spawns the prox projectile, and scales direct and splash damage.
Exact shared accuracy-hit predicate from g_weapon.c::LogAccuracyHit. Retail rejects non-damageable, self, non-client, same-team, and dead-or-frozen targets before callers increment accuracy stats.

Exact match for the master weapon dispatcher. It computes the quad factor, records per-weapon accuracy shots, resolves aim vectors and muzzle, routes into the weapon fire leaves, and derives chaingun spread from the shared playerState +0xe0 spin accumulator recovered in PM_Weapon.

functions.signature: void KamikazeRadiusDamage(vec3_t origin, gentity_t *attacker, float damage, float radius)
functions.comment: Retail Kamikaze damage sphere helper. It scans entities in the radius box, skips already-hit targets via the Kamikaze cooldown field, computes edge distance from each bounds box, and applies `MOD_KAMIKAZE` damage plus the 3-second hit suppression.

functions.signature: void KamikazeShockWave(vec3_t origin, gentity_t *attacker, float damage, float push, float radius)
functions.comment: Retail Kamikaze shockwave helper. The body mirrors the bounds-distance sweep from `KamikazeRadiusDamage`, applies `MOD_KAMIKAZE` damage, pushes client velocity outward, and stamps the Kamikaze shock cooldown.

Exact Kamikaze think routine from g_weapon.c. It advances the staged timer, emits the expanding shockwave and damage sphere, frees the explosion entity at the retail end time, and applies the earthquake-style client shake between ticks.

Retail Kamikaze bootstrapper. It spawns the `kamikaze` event entity, seeds `KamikazeDamage` as the periodic think, resolves the activator from the live player or body queue, kills the user when appropriate, and emits the broadcast Kamikaze global sound.

Descriptive retail-only callvote numeric validator. The helper is only reached from `Cmd_CallVote_f`, where it accepts a non-empty decimal token with at most one `.` and rejects longer or mixed-text inputs before timelimit and weapon-respawn votes proceed.

functions.signature: void Q_strncpyz(char *dest, const char *src, int destsize)
functions.comment: Exact shared safe-copy helper from q_shared.c::Q_strncpyz. The retail body preserves the NULL dest / NULL src / destsize < 1 Com_Error guards, then strncpy(dest, src, destsize - 1) and forces the trailing NUL.

Retail shared case-insensitive comparator used throughout cvar, info-string, and spawn parsing code. The fixed 99999-character bound and ASCII case-folding logic line it up with q_shared.c::Q_stricmp.

functions.signature: void Q_strcat(char *dest, int size, const char *src)
functions.comment: Exact shared bounded strcat helper from q_shared.c::Q_strcat. It measures the existing destination length, throws Com_Error on a pre-overflowed buffer, then appends through Q_strncpyz(dest + l1, src, size - l1).
Exact in-place color-code stripper from q_shared.c::Q_CleanStr. It skips color escape pairs, copies only printable ASCII bytes, and terminates the cleaned buffer in place.

functions.signature: int QDECL Com_sprintf(char *dest, int size, const char *fmt, ...)
functions.comment: Exact shared formatting helper from q_shared.c::Com_sprintf. It uses the 32000-byte bigbuffer, throws Com_Error on bigbuffer overflow, warns through Com_Printf on destination overflow, then copies out through Q_strncpyz.
Exact rotating two-buffer varargs formatter from q_shared.c::va. It toggles the static 32000-byte scratch buffer pair and returns the selected formatted buffer.

Exact info-string lookup helper from q_shared.c::Info_ValueForKey. It enforces BIG_INFO_STRING bounds, flips between the two static return buffers, parses \key\value pairs, and returns the matched value or the shared empty string.

Exact small info-string delete helper from q_shared.c::Info_RemoveKey. It enforces MAX_INFO_STRING bounds, rejects keys containing backslashes, walks \key\value pairs, and removes the matched segment in place.

functions.signature: void Info_SetValueForKey(char *s, const char *key, const char *value)
functions.comment: Exact small info-string mutator from q_shared.c::Info_SetValueForKey. It preserves the slash/semicolon/quote validation, remove-then-rebuild flow, and the final \key\value prepend when the result fits inside MAX_INFO_STRING.

Exact static gametype label helper from g_cmds.c::G_ForfeitGametypeName. It switches over gametype ids 0 through 12 to the Free For All/Duel/Race/.../Red Rover table and falls back to Unknown Gametype.

Descriptive retail-only rankings publisher anchored by the explicit PLAYER_MEDAL event name and MEDAL / TOTAL payload keys; callsites from kills, captures, assists, and post-round award checks all line up with a per-player medal emission helper.

functions.signature: void G_RankSendFlagStatus(gentity_t *ent, int team, int status)
functions.comment: Descriptive retail-only rankings publisher for the FLAG_STATUS payload. The preserved FLAG_ATBASE / FLAG_TAKEN / FLAG_DROPPED strings plus TEAM, STATUS, and optional STEAM_ID fields match the server-side flag-state event bridge.

Descriptive retail-only rankings publisher anchored by the PLAYER_RACECOMPLETE event name and the RACE_TIME, WEAPONS_USED, and STEAM_ID payload keys. The retail race-finish path calls it immediately after storing the completed run time.

functions.signature: void trap_RankReportInt(int index1, int index2, int key, int value, qboolean accum)
functions.comment: Exact rankings syscall wrapper from `g_syscalls.c::trap_RankReportInt`. Retail short-circuits when the rankings bridge is unavailable and otherwise forwards the integer-report payload through the native dispatch slot at offset `0x324`.

functions.signature: void G_RankSendPlayerSwitchTeam(gentity_t *ent, int oldTeam, int newTeam)
functions.comment: Descriptive retail-only rankings publisher identified by the PLAYER_SWITCHTEAM event name and the OLD_TEAM plus new-team payload fields. The helper is called from the retail team-change path immediately after the sessionTeam mutation.

Descriptive retail-only rankings death publisher anchored by the explicit PLAYER_DEATH / PLAYER_KILL payloads and the preserved KILLER, VICTIM, WEAPON, TEAMKILL, SUICIDE, AIRBORNE, STREAK, and MATCH_GUID fields. It also fans into the adjacent medal publisher when the retail kill-award conditions trigger.

Descriptive retail-only per-player rankings summary publisher anchored by the explicit PLAYER_STATS event name and the broad stat payload covering SCORE, KILLS, DEATHS, ACCURACY, MEDALS, item pickups, and per-weapon timing/usage totals. Retail calls it from match-end and team-switch teardown paths.

Descriptive retail-only rankings submitter called from `LogExit` after the per-player stats pass. The body builds the full match report payload, including EXIT_MSG, training and mutator flags, LAST_SCORER / LAST_TEAMSCORER, MATCH_GUID, and SERVER_TITLE, then hands it to the native `trap_SubmitMatchReport` bridge.

Descriptive retail-only rankings publisher anchored by the explicit MATCH_STARTED event name and the preserved PLAYERS roster, SERVER_TITLE, MATCH_GUID, and per-player STEAM_ID fields that are emitted when the ranked match bootstrap completes.
Exact QL-only G_CallSpawn dispatch-table constructor from g_team.c::SP_team_dom_point.

Descriptive retail-only warmup-deadline helper. It stores the new warmup timestamp, republishes `CS_WARMUP`, refreshes the adjacent auto-record controller, and drives `g_gameState` to `PRE_GAME`, `COUNT_DOWN`, or `IN_PROGRESS` based on the sign of the supplied deadline.

Descriptive retail-only inactivity helper wired to the `g_inactivityWarning` cvar callback. It walks connected clients and clears their per-client inactivity-warning latch so the countdown warning can be reissued under the new setting.

Descriptive retained ammo-slab sync helper behind the `g_instaGib` callback. It walks connected live clients and rewrites the playerstate ammo array either from the retail starting-ammo defaults or to `-1` sentinels, depending on whether the current infinite-ammo path is active.

Descriptive retail-only kill-path announcer anchored by the exact `print "%s%s killed the %s carrier!^7\n"` payload. It rejects self and same-team pairs, suppresses Harvester skull carriers, classifies the victim's carried Quad/Battle Suit/Regeneration/Haste/Invisibility powerup, and broadcasts the matching carrier kill banner.

Descriptive retail-only current-gametype wrapper around the shared objective-mode powerup-prefix predicate. HLIL reduces it to the no-arg classifier over `g_gametype` that the persistant-powerup pickup banner uses before choosing between neutral white and team-colored prefixes.

Exact slab allocator from g_mem.c::G_Alloc. Retail preserves the debug print and allocation-failure diagnostics, aligns every request to 32 bytes, advances the shared 4 MB game pool cursor, and returns the previous pool base on success.
Exact QL-only G_CallSpawn dispatch-table constructor from g_misc.c::SP_advertisement. Retail validates the `cellId` key, preserves the missing advert diagnostic, and then frees the entity.

Descriptive retail-only deferred `info_tour_point` finisher. It optionally hides overlapping ET_ITEM entities except persistant-powerup and team items, reapplies that hide path to matching tutorial targets, resolves `tourPointTarget` through `G_Find(..., FOFS(targetname), ...)`, preserves the missing tour-point diagnostic, and only then links the live entity.

Exact QL-only G_CallSpawn dispatch-table constructor anchored by the preserved `info_tour_point with no tourPointTarget...` diagnostics and the retail `radius`, `cvarValue`, `printChatTextTime`, `ignorePlayerLocation`, and `noise` spawnvars.

functions.signature: void TeleportPlayer(gentity_t *player, vec3_t origin, vec3_t angles)
functions.comment: Exact g_misc.c teleport helper. Retail emits the paired teleport temp events for non-spectators, unlinks before the telefrag check, reapplies view angles and the 400 ups launch velocity, toggles EF_TELEPORT_BIT, kills blocking entities at the destination, mirrors the new playerstate back into the entitystate, and only relinks non-spectators.

Shared g_utils.c free routine. Retail reuses this exact body for the `SP_info_null` / `SP_light` cleanup path and for the portal and proxmine free callbacks that collapse directly onto G_FreeEntity in the binary.

Exact g_misc.c portal-surface think callback. The body resolves the targeted `misc_portal_camera`, preserves the missing-target diagnostic, mirrors the camera spawnflags into the portal surface rotation state, copies the camera origin into `s.origin2`, and encodes the look direction from either the camera target or `s.angles` via DirToByte.
Exact G_CallSpawn dispatch-table constructor from g_misc.c::SP_misc_portal_surface.
Exact G_CallSpawn dispatch-table constructor from g_misc.c::SP_misc_portal_camera.
Exact g_misc.c shooter use callback. Retail resolves enemy-or-movedir aim, applies the two-axis random spread from ent->random, dispatches grenade/rocket/plasma fire, and emits EV_FIRE_WEAPON.
Delayed shooter target resolver from g_misc.c. It picks ent->target through G_PickTarget and clears the scheduled think state once the enemy pointer is seeded.

Exact shooter bootstrap helper from g_misc.c. It binds Use_Shooter, registers the weapon item, computes movedir from angles, normalizes the random spread through sin(degrees), and delays target resolution when ent->target is set.
Exact G_CallSpawn dispatch-table constructor from g_misc.c::SP_shooter_rocket.
Exact G_CallSpawn dispatch-table constructor from g_misc.c::SP_shooter_plasma.
Exact G_CallSpawn dispatch-table constructor from g_misc.c::SP_shooter_grenade.

Exact portal-destination drop helper from g_misc.c. It spawns the hi_portal destination entity, copies the player bounds and angles, schedules free cleanup, assigns a fresh portal sequence, and restores the Portal holdable.

Exact g_misc.c portal touch callback. It rejects dead or non-client entities, tosses carried flags, finds the matching hi_portal destination by count, and telefrags the user when no destination survives.
Portal source arming helper from g_misc.c. Retail enables PortalTouch on the entity and converts the one-second warmup into the normal two-minute cleanup timer.

Exact portal-source drop helper from g_misc.c. It spawns the hi_portal source entity, transfers the saved portal sequence from the client, arms PortalEnable after one second, and caches the destination origin into pos1 when the paired exit already exists.

Shared missile ricochet helper from g_missile.c. It reflects the trajectory delta across the impact plane, applies the EF_BOUNCE_HALF dampening and stop test, and rewrites trBase/trTime from the current origin.

Exact g_missile.c explosion core. Retail evaluates the current trajectory, snaps and sets origin, emits EV_MISSILE_MISS, marks freeAfterEvent, applies splash damage and accuracy bookkeeping, and relinks the entity.
Exact proxmine explosion wrapper from g_missile.c. It forwards into G_ExplodeMissile and frees the spawned trigger volume through mine->activator when present.

functions.signature: void ProximityMine_Die(gentity_t *ent, gentity_t *inflictor, gentity_t *attacker, int damage, int mod)
functions.comment: Minimal proxmine die callback from g_missile.c. Retail schedules ProximityMine_Explode on the next frame after the mine takes lethal damage.
Exact proxmine trigger touch callback from g_missile.c. It applies the spherical distance test, same-team rejection, CanDamage gating, and then arms the parent mine with EV_PROXIMITY_MINE_TRIGGER.

Exact g_missile.c proxmine arming helper. It switches the mine into the ticking damageable state, seeds the timeout, creates the proxmine_trigger child entity, and links the trigger back through ent->activator.
Player-stuck proxmine detonation helper from g_missile.c. It clears EF_TICKING, handles the invulnerability juiced path, otherwise snaps the mine onto the victim and tailcalls G_ExplodeMissile.

Exact player-stick helper from g_missile.c. It handles the already-ticking stack-up case, otherwise hides the mine on the victim, marks EF_TICKING, and schedules ProximityMine_ExplodeOnPlayer with the retail invulnerability-aware delay split.

Exact g_missile.c impact handler. Retail preserves the bounce and invulnerability branches, the direct-damage and accuracy path, the proxmine player-or-surface stick logic, the grapple hook handling, and the shared missile event/explosion flow.
Exact plat top-touch helper. Retail only reacts to living clients, and when the plat is already at POS2 it bumps `nextthink` out by one second to delay the return leg.
Exact center-trigger callback for plats. It only reacts to clients and only starts the parent mover when the plat is still resting at POS1.

Exact plat-trigger constructor from g_mover.c. Retail spawns the `plat_trigger` helper, applies the same 33-unit XY inset and 8-unit Z cap with the 1-unit fallback for narrow platforms, then links the resulting trigger volume.
Exact G_CallSpawn dispatch-table constructor from g_mover.c::SP_func_plat.

Descriptive retail-only keyed-button touch gate. It only reacts to clients, only while the button is still resting at POS1, and requires the matching silver/master or gold/master key bit before forwarding into `Use_BinaryMover`.
Exact button touch callback from g_mover.c. Retail only reacts to clients and triggers the binary mover while the button is still at POS1.
Exact G_CallSpawn dispatch-table constructor from g_mover.c::SP_func_button.
Exact train wait-complete helper. Retail resumes the train by stamping `trTime = level.time` and restoring `TR_LINEAR_STOP` after a path-corner wait expires.

Exact train corner-arrival handler from g_mover.c. Retail advances `nextTrain`, fires the corner targets, rebuilds pos1/pos2 and `trDuration` from the next path pair, copies the corner loop sound, starts the mover toward the next corner, and schedules `Think_BeginMoving` when the corner carries a wait.

Exact train-path linker from g_mover.c. The retail body resolves the first target, preserves the `func_train at %s with an unfound target` and both `Train corner ...` diagnostics, links every path_corner through `nextTrain`, and then tailcalls `Reached_Train` to start motion.
Exact G_CallSpawn dispatch-table constructor from g_mover.c::SP_path_corner.
Exact G_CallSpawn dispatch-table constructor from g_mover.c::SP_func_train.
Exact G_CallSpawn dispatch-table constructor from g_mover.c::SP_func_static.
Exact G_CallSpawn dispatch-table constructor from g_mover.c::SP_func_rotating.
Exact G_CallSpawn dispatch-table constructor from g_mover.c::SP_func_bobbing.

functions.signature: int G_FindConfigstringIndex(char *name, int start, int max, qboolean create)

functions.signature: void G_Sound(gentity_t *ent, int channel, int soundIndex)
functions.comment: Exact temp-entity sound helper from g_utils.c. Retail emits `EV_GENERAL_SOUND` at `ent->r.currentOrigin` through `G_TempEntity` and stores `soundIndex` in `s.eventParm`; the `channel` parameter is preserved but unused.
Shared entity string-field iterator from g_utils.c. Retail walks active entities from the post-from cursor, reads the requested FOFS string field, and returns the next case-insensitive match.
Random target selector from g_utils.c. It preserves the NULL-target diagnostic, collects up to MAXCHOICES matching targetname entities through G_Find, and chooses one with rand().
Exact g_utils.c target-dispatch loop. Retail iterates matching targetname entities through G_Find, warns on self-use, invokes each use callback, and preserves the entity-was-removed diagnostic.

Exact movedir helper from g_utils.c. Retail preserves the same special `VEC_UP` and `VEC_DOWN` angle sentinels, falls back to `AngleVectors` for ordinary yaw input, and clears the source angles after converting them into a direction.

Exact yaw extractor from g_utils.c. It handles the zero-vector fast path, the straight-left and straight-right special cases, the `atan2( vec[YAW], vec[PITCH] ) * 180 / M_PI` conversion, and the final wrap into the `[0, 360)` range.

Exact entity allocator from g_utils.c. It reuses free slots outside the recent-freetime window, opens a new entity slot through `trap_LocateGameData` when needed, and preserves the `G_Spawn: no free entities` fatal path.

Exact temporary-event spawner from g_utils.c. Retail allocates a new entity through `G_Spawn`, marks it as an auto-free event entity, snaps the origin for network efficiency, and links it for the PVS path.

Exact telefrag helper from g_utils.c. It builds the destination bounds from the client's origin plus entity mins/maxs, enumerates touches through `trap_EntitiesInBox`, and applies unconditional telefrag damage to overlapping clients.

functions.signature: void G_AddPredictableEvent(gentity_t *ent, int event, int eventParm)
functions.comment: Exact predictable-event helper from g_utils.c. Retail bails when the entity has no client and otherwise forwards the event plus parm into `BG_AddPredictableEventToPlayerstate` against that client's playerstate.

functions.signature: void G_AddEvent(gentity_t *ent, int event, int eventParm)
functions.comment: Exact event helper core from g_utils.c. Retail preserves the same event-bit rollover and client-vs-entity storage split as source, publishing into `client->ps.externalEvent` / `externalEventParm` or `ent->s.event` / `eventParm` and stamping `ent->eventTime = level.time`; the source-side zero-event warning is hoisted out of this leaf.

Exact fixed-position helper from g_utils.c. It copies the supplied origin into `s.pos.trBase`, marks the trajectory stationary with zeroed time and delta, and mirrors the same coordinates into `r.currentOrigin`.

Descriptive retail-only cleaned-name helper keyed by client number. It reads `CS_PLAYERS + clientNum`, extracts the `n` field, strips color formatting through `Q_CleanStr`, and copies the result into the fixed 40-byte destination buffer used by retail capture and rankings messages.

Descriptive retail-only cleaned-name wrapper keyed by `gclient_t *`. It derives the client index from `level.clients`, then follows the same `CS_PLAYERS` -> `Info_ValueForKey( ..., "n" )` -> `Q_CleanStr` copy path as `G_CleanClientNameFromClientNum`.

Descriptive retail-only netname accessor. It copies `level.clients[clientNum].pers.netname` into a shared static buffer and returns that buffer without color stripping, matching the retail helper used by rename, chat, timeout, and flag-announcement prints.

functions.signature: void CalcMuzzlePoint(gentity_t *ent, vec3_t forward, vec3_t right, vec3_t up, vec3_t muzzlePoint)
functions.comment: Exact muzzle-point helper from g_weapon.c. Retail derives forward vectors from `client->ps.viewangles`, seeds the muzzle from `ent->s.pos.trBase`, adds `client->ps.viewheight`, and applies the same crouched-vs-standing forward offset before publishing the shared muzzle vector; this leaf also covers the no-op `CalcMuzzlePointOrigin` variant folded into the same implementation.

functions.signature: void G_BounceProjectile(vec3_t start, vec3_t impact, vec3_t dir, vec3_t endout)
functions.comment: Exact bounce-reflection helper from g_weapon.c. The body reconstructs `impact - start`, reflects that vector across the supplied direction with the same `-2 * dot` term, normalizes the result, and projects the bounced endpoint 8192 units from the impact point.

Exact network-rounding helper from g_weapon.c. It walks the three vector components, truncates each coordinate toward zero, and bumps the result upward when needed so the snapped endpoint stays on the `to` side of the reference vector.

Descriptive retail-only persistent-powerup pickup-message helper. It compares the picked-up rune against the cached red and blue base origins, chooses the matching color prefix, and emits either the global pickup announcement or the opponent-side-only pickup notice used by the retail Pickup_PersistantPowerup flow.

Descriptive retail-only item-powerup cache initializer. It resolves the red and blue flag-base entities, caches their origins, clears the per-item availability counters, and rebuilds those counts from active map entities for the dynamic persistent-powerup and damaged-health systems.

Descriptive retail-only rankings-state reset helper. It zeroes the per-client rankings slab, seeds the timestamp fields from `level.time`, and initializes the session token reused by the retail PLAYER_CONNECT and PLAYER_DISCONNECT publishers.

Exact retail rankings connect publisher anchored by the explicit PLAYER_CONNECT event name. It builds the TIME, WARMUP, MATCH_GUID, NAME, and STEAM_ID payload and emits that event from the ClientConnect path.

Exact retail rankings disconnect publisher anchored by the explicit PLAYER_DISCONNECT event name. It mirrors the connect payload keys, reuses the per-client session token, and is called from ClientDisconnect before the client is unlinked.

Descriptive retail-only rankings accumulator. It advances the total active-time counter plus the current-weapon bucket from the last timestamp, skips spectator-style states, and refreshes the timestamp consumed by later disconnect and score-message publishers.

Descriptive retail-only rankings helper. It picks the preferred-weapon index from the enabled-weapon mask and the accumulated per-weapon time totals, then feeds that result into the extended ranked score-message builders.

functions.signature: void *StdStringAssignRange(void *str, const char *text, unsigned int length)

functions.signature: void *StdStringAssignSubstr(void *dest, const void *source, unsigned int startIndex, unsigned int count)
functions.comment: Descriptive retained string-runtime helper. Standalone retained string-runtime fragment split out of `StdStringGrow`. It copies the preserved prefix into the replacement allocation when needed, releases the prior heap buffer, installs the new buffer/capacity/length triplet, and rewrites the trailing NUL terminator.

functions.signature: void *StdStringGrow(void *str, unsigned int requestedSize, unsigned int preserveLength)
functions.comment: Descriptive retained string-runtime growth helper. It picks a new capacity using the MSVC small-string and 1.5x growth rules, allocates a replacement buffer, preserves the requested prefix length, frees the old heap storage when necessary, and terminates the resized string.

Compiler-generated catch-all funclet for `StdStringGrow`. It releases the temporary heap buffer captured in the EH frame, restores the destination string to the empty small-string state, and then rethrows.

functions.signature: void *StdStringErase(void *str, unsigned int offset, unsigned int count)
functions.comment: Descriptive retained string-runtime erase helper. It validates the erase offset, clamps the removal count to the remaining string length, slides the surviving suffix down with `memmove`, updates the stored length, and rewrites the trailing NUL.

Exact retained MSVC copy constructor for `std::runtime_error`. It copy-constructs the base `std::exception` payload and then restores the `std::runtime_error` vftable reused by the adjacent throw-info record.

Exact JsonCpp string-release helper from `json_value.cpp`. The body returns immediately for null pointers and otherwise frees the supplied heap buffer.

Exact retained scalar-deleting destructor for `Json::ValueAllocator`. The abstract base restores its own vftable and conditionally deletes the object when the compiler-supplied delete flag requests it.

Descriptive allocator accessor for the embedded Json runtime. It lazily installs the process-global `Json::DefaultValueAllocator` singleton, registers the shared atexit cleanup, and returns the allocator object reused by the adjacent duplicate and release vtable calls.

Exact destructor for `Json::Value::CommentInfo`. It checks for a live comment pointer and releases that buffer through the shared Json allocator path.

Exact `Json::Value::CommentInfo` setter from `json_value.cpp`. Retail releases any previous buffer, enforces the retained `Comments must start with /` assertion, and duplicates the new text through the shared allocator.

Exact copy constructor for `Json::Value::CZString`. It duplicates allocator-owned member-name strings, preserves non-owned static references without copying, and normalizes the duplication-policy tag in the destination.
Exact destructor for `Json::Value::CZString`. The leaf only releases the stored key string when the duplication-policy field marks that string as allocator-owned.

Exact `Json::Value( const char * )` string constructor from `json_value.cpp`. It tags the destination as an allocated string value, clears the optional comment pointer, and duplicates the incoming NUL-terminated text through the shared Json allocator.

Descriptive retail-only Json string-slot setter. It tags the target value as an allocated string, clears the optional comment pointer, duplicates the incoming NUL-terminated text through the shared Json allocator, and returns the duplicated buffer pointer.

Exact Json value copy constructor from the embedded JsonCpp runtime. It copies scalar payloads directly, duplicates owned strings, clones map-backed array and object storage through `JsonValueCopyMapStorage`, and deep-copies the optional three-slot comment array.

Exact `Json::Value::~Value()` body from the embedded JsonCpp runtime. HLIL shows it switching on the tagged Json payload kind, releasing allocator-owned strings through the default allocator vtable, recursively tearing down map-backed array/object storage, and then freeing the optional three-slot comment array.

Exact `Json::Value::operator=( const Value & )` body from `json_value.cpp`. Retail copy-constructs the temporary `Value other`, swaps payload/tag/comment state into the destination, and then destroys the displaced temporary value.

Exact `Json::Value::operator==( const Value & ) const` predicate from `json_value.cpp`. HLIL compares the tagged value kinds first, then handles int/uint/real/string/bool directly and falls through to the map-backed array/object comparison path for the container kinds.
