Exact `Json::Value::operator[]( const char * )` body from `json_value.cpp`, with the private `resolveReference( key, false )` helper inlined by retail optimization. When the incoming value is null it promotes it to the map-backed object kind, then performs the CZString-style key lookup/insert path and returns the nested member slot for the requested key.

Exact `Json::Value::append` helper from `json_value.cpp`. It computes the current array size, resolves the next numeric slot through `JsonValueOperatorIndexArrayIndex`, assigns the incoming element into that slot, and returns the inserted element reference.

Descriptive retail-only constructor for the map-backed Json storage header. It allocates the 0x30-byte red-black-tree sentinel node, points the root/left/right links back at that sentinel, and seeds the empty-tree color flags.

Descriptive retail-only red-black-tree erase helper used by the map-backed Json container. It validates the iterator node, unlinks the target, performs the standard tree-fixup rotations and recoloring, destroys the embedded Json value payload, and decrements the stored member count.

Descriptive retail-only copy-constructor helper for the map-backed Json storage used by array/object values. It allocates a fresh sentinel node, initializes the empty tree header, and then clones the source member tree through the adjacent iterator/copy path.
Compiler-generated catch-all funclet for `JsonValueClearMapRange`. It resumes range destruction from the current iterator state through the parent clear helper and then rethrows.

Descriptive retail-only tree-range clear helper for the map-backed Json storage. It special-cases full-map clears into the recursive node-destroy path, otherwise walks the selected range and repeatedly erases nodes through `JsonValueEraseMemberNode`.

Descriptive retail-only recursive node destructor for the map-backed Json container. It descends the tree, destroys each embedded Json value plus allocator-owned key storage, and frees every non-sentinel node.

Descriptive internal lower-bound search for the map-backed Json storage. It walks the red-black tree in `CZString` key order and returns the first node whose key is not less than the requested member/index key, or the sentinel when the search runs off the tree.

Descriptive internal left-rotation helper for the map-backed Json red-black tree. It promotes the current node's right child, rewires parent and root links through the storage header, and returns the rotated subtree root.

Descriptive internal right-rotation helper for the map-backed Json red-black tree. It promotes the current node's left child, rewires parent and root links through the storage header, and returns the rotated subtree root.
Descriptive tiny helper for the map-backed Json storage header. It frees the sentinel-node allocation stored at `storage + 4`; the owning caller handles any member-node teardown separately.
Descriptive internal rightmost-node walk for the map-backed Json tree. The erase helper uses it when it needs to rebuild the sentinel's cached end-iterator predecessor from a replacement subtree.

Descriptive internal `CZString` ordering predicate for the map-backed Json storage. It applies the same string-versus-index ordering used by the lower-bound and insert helpers so hinted inserts and duplicate-key checks stay aligned.

Descriptive internal in-order successor helper for the map-backed Json tree. It advances an iterator node pointer to the next key in sorted order, descending into the right subtree or climbing parents as needed.

Descriptive internal in-order predecessor helper for the map-backed Json tree. It supports both normal iterator decrement and the `end()` case by falling back to the cached rightmost node in the storage sentinel.

Descriptive internal whole-tree clone helper for the map-backed Json storage. It duplicates the source root through the recursive subtree copier, copies the stored member count, and then rebuilds the leftmost and rightmost boundary links cached in the destination sentinel.

Descriptive internal recursive subtree clone for the map-backed Json storage. It constructs a destination node from the source key/value pair, preserves the source color bit, and recursively recreates the left and right child links under the supplied parent.

Compiler-generated catch-all funclet for `JsonValueMapCloneSubtree`. When recursive child construction throws, it destroys the partially cloned subtree through `JsonValueDestroyMapNodes` and then rethrows.

Descriptive internal Json map-node constructor. It allocates a 0x30-byte tree node, seeds the left/right/parent links from the map sentinel, copy-constructs the embedded `CZString` key, and copy-constructs the sibling `Json::Value` payload.
Compiler-generated catch-all funclet for `JsonValueMapNodeConstruct`. It frees the 0x30-byte node allocation recorded in the EH frame and then rethrows after key or value construction fails.

Descriptive internal hinted-insert helper for the map-backed Json storage. It checks the supplied iterator hint against the pending node key, routes good hints into the red-black-tree link path, and otherwise falls back to the general unique-insert helper.

Descriptive internal unique-insert helper for the map-backed Json storage. It walks the `CZString` key order, inserts the pending node when no equivalent key exists, or destroys and frees that pending node before returning the existing node on duplicates.

functions.signature: void *JsonValueMapLinkNode(void *parent, void *storage, void *out, qboolean insertLeft, void *node)

Exact directional-quantization helper from q_math.c. It scans the 162-entry `bytedirs` table, picks the highest dot-product match for the supplied unit vector, and returns the compact direction index used by events such as gibs and impacts.

functions.signature: void ProjectPointOnPlane(vec3_t dst, const vec3_t p, const vec3_t normal)

functions.signature: float RadiusFromBounds(const vec3_t mins, const vec3_t maxs)

functions.comment: Exact Euler-basis helper from q_math.c. The body computes the sine/cosine pairs for yaw, pitch, and roll, then fills the optional forward/right/up outputs with the retail direction basis.

Exact shared parser helper from q_shared.c::COM_ParseExt. The retail body skips whitespace plus // and /* */ comments, tracks line breaks through `com_lines`, preserves the quoted-token path, and returns the shared static token buffer.

Descriptive retail-only rankings stringifier anchored by the explicit SHOTGUN / ROCKET / LIGHTNING / SWITCHTEAM / THAW / LIGHTNING_DISCHARGE literals plus the OTHER_DEATH fallback. The adjacent PLAYER_DEATH publisher uses it for the means-of-death payload naming.

Descriptive retail-only rankings bucket resolver. It collapses means-of-death ids onto the reduced weapon index space reused by the kill/death publishers and the adjacent per-weapon damage accumulators.

Descriptive retail-only rankings weapon-name helper anchored by the explicit GAUNTLET / MACHINEGUN / SHOTGUN / ROCKET / LIGHTNING / RAILGUN / PLASMA / NAILGUN / PROXMINE / CHAINGUN literals and the OTHER_WEAPON fallback.

Descriptive retail-only rankings powerup-name helper anchored by the explicit RED_FLAG / BLUE_FLAG / QUAD / BATTLESUIT / HASTE / INVULNERABILITY / GUARD / DOUBLER / ARMOR_REGEN literals and the OTHER_POWERUP fallback.

functions.signature: static void G_RankCountAliveDeadByTeam(int deadCounts[TEAM_NUM_TEAMS], int aliveCounts[TEAM_NUM_TEAMS])
functions.comment: Descriptive retail-only rankings helper. It zeroes the dead and alive team-count arrays, scans connected client entities by `sessionTeam`, and feeds the `TEAM_ALIVE`, `TEAM_DEAD`, `OTHER_TEAM_ALIVE`, and `OTHER_TEAM_DEAD` payload fields used by the retail kill and death publishers.

functions.signature: void G_RankAccumulateDamage(gentity_t *victim, gentity_t *attacker, int meansOfDeath, int damage)
functions.comment: Descriptive retail-only rankings damage accumulator. It derives the reduced weapon bucket through `G_RankMeansOfDeathWeapon`, updates the victim's total and per-weapon damage-taken counters plus cached armor/health snapshots, and credits the attacker's damage-given buckets when the hit is not self-damage or filtered team damage.

functions.signature: void G_RankSendRoundOver(int round, int winningTeam, qboolean draw)
functions.comment: Descriptive retail-only rankings publisher anchored by the explicit ROUND_OVER event name and the TIME, WARMUP, MATCH_GUID, ROUND, and TEAM_WON payload keys. Retail round-resolution paths pass the round index, winning team, and draw flag into this helper at round end.
